Specific location code where the container was picked up. May be berth number, yard block-bay-row-tier, gate lane, or rail track identifier.
Classification of the origin facility where the move started. Defines the source context for the move.
Detailed position coordinates at origin. For vessel: bay-row-tier from BAPLIE. For yard: block-bay-row-tier. For gate: lane number.
Scheduled date and time when the move was planned to begin. Used for operational planning and schedule adherence measurement.

UN/LOCODE 5-character code for the port where the cargo was originally loaded onto the vessel. Origin port in the shipping journey.
Required temperature setting in degrees Celsius for refrigerated containers. Null for non-reefer containers.
Security seal number affixed to the container. Used for cargo security verification and tamper detection.

Specific stage or leg of the move indicating origin and destination facility types. Defines the operational context of the move. UN/LOCODE for intermediate transhipment port if cargo is being transferred between vessels. Null for direct shipments.
Four-digit UN number identifying the dangerous goods substance (e.g., UN1203 for gasoline). Required for IMDG compliance.
reference: UN Recommendations on Transport of Dangerous Goods

business_glossary_term: Annual Throughput Capacity (TEU - Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit)
description: The estimated annual container throughput capacity of this berth measured in TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit). Applicable only to container berths. Used for capacity planning, port expansion initiatives, and commercial planning. Null for non-container berths.

The unique identifier for this berth in the AVEVA Marine Engineering system. Used for integration with port design, infrastructure planning, and engineering documentation. Links berth master data to detailed engineering models and drawings.
The common name or designation of the berth, often referencing location or purpose (e.g., Container Terminal North Berth 1, Bulk Cargo Berth A).
The official berth number or code assigned by the port authority. This is the externally-known identifier used in vessel planning, NAVIS N4, TOPS Expert TOS, and port documentation.

Classification of the berth based on the primary cargo or vessel type it serves. Container berths handle containerized cargo, bulk berths handle dry or liquid bulk commodities, RoRo (Roll-on Roll-off) berths serve vehicle carriers, tanker berths handle liquid cargo vessels, and cruise berths accommodate passenger vessels.

The total number of mooring bollards installed along this berth. Bollards are fixed posts to which vessel mooring lines are secured. The count and spacing of bollards determine the mooring capacity and configuration options.

The Safe Working Load (SWL) rating of the mooring bollards at this berth, measured in metric tonnes. SWL represents the maximum load that can be safely applied to a bollard during normal mooring operations. All bollards at a berth typically share the same SWL rating.

Boolean flag indicating whether this berth has direct or immediate proximity to a CFS (Container Freight Station) for LCL (Less than Container Load) cargo consolidation and deconsolidation operations.
The date when this berth was officially commissioned and became operational for vessel berthing. Used for asset lifecycle tracking, depreciation calculations, and capacity planning analysis.

The current physical condition of the fender system based on the most recent inspection. Condition ratings range from excellent (new or like-new) to critical (immediate replacement required). Sourced from inspection records in Maximo Asset Management.

The total energy absorption capacity of the fender system, measured in kilojoules (kJ). This represents the maximum kinetic energy that the fender system can safely absorb during vessel berthing without damage.

The maximum reaction force that the fender system can exert on a berthing vessel, measured in kilonewtons (kN). This is a critical safety parameter for vessel berthing operations and is used in vessel planning to ensure compatibility.

The type of fender system installed at this berth. Fenders absorb the kinetic energy of berthing vessels and protect both the vessel and the quay wall structure. Common types include pneumatic (air-filled rubber), foam-filled, buckling (energy-absorbing steel), cylindrical, cone, cell, arch, and unit fenders. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pneumatic|foam_filled|buckling|cylindrical|cone|cell|arch|unit — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

business_glossary_term: International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Compliant Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether this berth meets ISPS (International Ship and Port Facility Security) Code requirements. ISPS compliance is mandatory for berths handling international vessels and includes security fencing, access control, surveillance, and lighting.

The date of the most recent dredging operation to maintain the required water depth alongside this berth. Dredging is performed periodically to remove sediment accumulation and maintain draft capacity. Sourced from dredging records.

The date of the most recent comprehensive structural and equipment inspection of this berth. Inspections cover quay wall integrity, fender condition, mooring equipment, and safety systems. Sourced from Maximo Asset Management inspection records.

The geographic latitude coordinate of the berth center point in decimal degrees. Used for vessel navigation, VTS (Vessel Traffic Service) integration, and GIS-based port layout planning in AVEVA Marine Engineering.
The total usable length of the berth measured in meters along the quay wall. This is the maximum continuous length available for vessel mooring and determines the LOA (Length Overall) capacity.

The maximum Length Overall (LOA) of a vessel that can be safely accommodated at this berth, measured in meters. This is a critical constraint for vessel planning and berth allocation in NAVIS N4 and TOPS Expert TOS.

The geographic longitude coordinate of the berth center point in decimal degrees. Used for vessel navigation, VTS (Vessel Traffic Service) integration, and GIS-based port layout planning in AVEVA Marine Engineering.

The maximum vessel draft (depth of the vessel below the waterline) that can be safely accommodated at this berth, measured in meters. This is constrained by the water depth alongside the berth and channel depth.

The maximum Deadweight Tonnage (DWT) of a vessel that can be accommodated at this berth, measured in metric tonnes. DWT represents the total weight a vessel can safely carry including cargo, fuel, crew, and provisions.

Comma-separated list of mooring fitting types installed at this berth beyond bollards, such as hooks, rings, cleats, or quick-release hooks. Different vessel types and mooring configurations require different fitting types.

The scheduled date for the next planned preventive maintenance activity for this berth. Preventive maintenance includes fender replacement, bollard inspection, quay wall repairs, and equipment servicing. Sourced from Maximo Asset Management maintenance plans.

Current operational state of the berth. Operational berths are available for vessel scheduling, under_maintenance berths are temporarily unavailable, out_of_service berths are closed for extended periods, planned berths are under construction, and decommissioned berths are permanently retired.
Boolean flag indicating whether this berth has direct rail connection for intermodal cargo transfer. Rail connectivity is critical for inland container depots (ICD) and bulk cargo operations.
The timestamp when this berth record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and record lifecycle tracking.
The timestamp when this berth record was last modified. Updated whenever any attribute value changes. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit purposes.

Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, operational constraints, or historical information about this berth that do not fit into structured fields. Used by port operations and planning teams.

The number of shore-based cranes (STS - Ship-to-Shore cranes, QC - Quay Cranes, or MHC - Mobile Harbour Cranes) assigned to or available at this berth for cargo handling operations. Critical for container terminal berths and determines throughput capacity.

Boolean flag indicating whether shore power (cold ironing) is available at this berth. Shore power allows vessels to shut down auxiliary engines while berthed, reducing emissions and noise. Increasingly required for environmental compliance.
reference: ISO 14001 Environmental Management, IMO MARPOL Annex VI

description: The total electrical power capacity available for shore power connection at this berth, measured in kilowatts (kW). Null if shore power is not available. Typical container vessel shore power requirements range from 1,000 to 10,000 kW.

Boolean flag indicating whether this berth has operational constraints due to tidal conditions. True indicates that vessel movements (arrival, departure, or cargo operations) are restricted to specific tidal windows.

The average tidal range (difference between high tide and low tide) at this berth location, measured in meters. Tidal constraints impact vessel scheduling, particularly for vessels with drafts close to the maximum depth.

Boolean flag indicating whether this berth has direct or immediate proximity to covered warehouse facilities. Important for general cargo and break-bulk operations requiring weather-protected storage.

The measured water depth alongside the berth at chart datum, measured in meters. This depth is maintained through regular dredging operations and determines the maximum draft capacity. Sourced from channel depth surveys and dredging records.

Distinctive light pattern or characteristic displayed by the aid (e.g., 'Fl(2)R 10s', 'Iso G 4s', 'Q(6)+LFl 15s'). Describes the rhythm, color, and period of the light signal. Null for non-illuminated aids.

description: Primary color of the light emitted by the navigational aid. Null for non-illuminated aids. Color coding follows IALA conventions for lateral and cardinal marks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: red|green|white|yellow|blue|amber|orange — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Geographic longitude coordinate of the navigational aid in decimal degrees. Positive values indicate East, negative values indicate West. Precision to 7 decimal places provides sub-meter accuracy.
Name of the manufacturer or supplier of the navigational aid equipment. Used for warranty tracking and spare parts procurement.
Nine-digit MMSI number assigned to AIS-equipped navigational aids for automatic identification and tracking. Null for non-AIS aids.
Manufacturer's model or product number for the navigational aid. Used for technical specifications lookup and spare parts ordering.
Method by which the navigational aid is secured or anchored (e.g., sinker weight, chain mooring, pile-mounted, fixed to structure). Null or 'none' for shore-based fixed structures.
Scheduled date for the next mandatory inspection or maintenance activity. Used for preventive maintenance planning and regulatory compliance.
reference: IALA Recommendation O-109 on Availability Objectives

description: Nominal range of the light in nautical miles under standard atmospheric conditions (meteorological visibility of 10 nautical miles). Null for non-illuminated aids.
reference: IALA Recommendation E-200-2 on Marine Signal Lights

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, or remarks about the navigational aid (e.g., seasonal operation, temporary changes, known issues).
Current operational state of the navigational aid. Indicates whether the aid is functioning and available for navigation support.
reference: International Maritime Organization (IMO) SOLAS Chapter V

description: Primary power source used to operate the navigational aid. Null for passive aids (daymarks, unlit buoys). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mains_electric|solar|battery|wind|diesel_generator|gas|hybrid|none — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

description: Single-letter Morse code identifier transmitted by the radar beacon (RACON) when interrogated by ship radar. Null for aids without RACON capability.
Indicates whether the navigational aid is equipped with radar-reflective material or retroreflective tape to enhance radar and visual detection.
Name of the organization or authority responsible for the maintenance and operation of this navigational aid (e.g., port authority, coast guard, hydrographic office).

description: Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer to this specific navigational aid unit. Used for warranty claims and asset tracking.

description: Type of audible signal device installed on the navigational aid for use in reduced visibility conditions. Null or 'none' for aids without sound signals.
reference: IALA Recommendation E-108 on Sound Signals

description: Total physical height of the navigational aid structure from base to top in meters. Used for clearance calculations and physical planning.
Shape of the topmark (daymark) mounted on top of the aid for daytime identification. Follows IALA conventions for cardinal and lateral marks. Null or 'none' for aids without topmarks.

Radius of the watch circle (swing radius) for floating aids, indicating the maximum distance the aid can drift from its charted position due to mooring scope and tidal/current effects. Null for fixed aids.
reference: IALA Recommendation O-109 on Availability Objectives

Geographic service corridors, routes, or regions covered by the haulier's transport network (e.g., port-to-ICD, regional shuttle, cross-border).

reference: UN/LOCODE - United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations

description: Official registered name of the ICD or CFS facility as recognized by port authorities and logistics partners.
reference: International Association of Ports and Harbors (IAPH) - Port Facility Naming Standards

description: Classification of the inland facility: ICD (Inland Container Depot) for full container handling, CFS (Container Freight Station) for LCL consolidation/deconsolidation, or HYBRID for facilities offering both services.
reference: International Maritime Organization (IMO) - Facility Classification Standards

description: Indicates whether the facility provides FCL (Full Container Load) handling services. True if the facility can receive, store, and dispatch full containers; False otherwise.
reference: International Maritime Organization (IMO) - Container Handling Standards

business_glossary_term: International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) License Number
description: Official license or certification number for dangerous goods handling issued by maritime safety authorities. Required when dangerous_goods_certified = True. Used in hazmat compliance reporting.

business_glossary_term: International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Code Compliant
description: Indicates whether the facility meets ISPS Code security requirements for port facilities. True if ISPS certification is current; False otherwise. Mandatory for facilities handling international cargo.
reference: International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Code

description: User identifier or system account that last modified this facility record. Used for audit trails and data governance accountability.
System timestamp when this facility record was last updated. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance.
Geographic latitude coordinate of the facility in decimal degrees format. Used for GPS navigation, route optimization, and geospatial analytics. Range: -90.0000000 to +90.0000000.

business_glossary_term: Less than Container Load (LCL) Service Available
description: Indicates whether the facility provides LCL (Less than Container Load) consolidation and deconsolidation services. True if the facility operates as a CFS for break-bulk cargo; False otherwise.
reference: International Maritime Organization (IMO) - Container Handling Standards

description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the facility in decimal degrees format. Used for GPS navigation, route optimization, and geospatial analytics. Range: -180.0000000 to +180.0000000.

Standard operating hours of the facility in local time, typically formatted as day ranges and time ranges (e.g., 'Mon-Fri 08:00-18:00, Sat 08:00-14:00'). Used for truck appointment scheduling and gate operations planning.

Current operational state of the facility in the port's intermodal network. ACTIVE indicates full operations, INACTIVE for temporary closure, SUSPENDED for regulatory or safety holds, UNDER_CONSTRUCTION for facilities being built, DECOMMISSIONED for permanently closed facilities.
Primary email address for operational coordination and communication with the facility operator. Used for EDI notifications, booking confirmations, and service requests.
Primary telephone number for operational coordination with the facility operator, including country code. Used for urgent operational matters and emergency contact.
reference: International Telecommunication Union (ITU) - E.164 Numbering Plan

description: Postal or ZIP code for the facility address. Used for mail delivery, geographic analysis, and logistics routing optimization.
reference: Universal Postal Union (UPU) - Postal Code Standards

description: Indicates whether the facility has direct rail access for intermodal container transfer. True if rail siding or terminal connection exists; False for truck-only facilities. Critical for rail operations planning and modal shift strategies.
reference: International Union of Railways (UIC) - Intermodal Standards

description: Number of electrical plug-in points available for refrigerated containers (reefers) requiring temperature control. Used for cold chain logistics planning and reefer container allocation.
reference: International Maritime Organization (IMO) - Reefer Container Standards

description: Current ISPS security level of the facility. LEVEL_1 for normal operations, LEVEL_2 for heightened security risk, LEVEL_3 for imminent security threat. Determines access control and security protocols.
reference: International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Code

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Turnaround Time in Hours
description: Contractual maximum turnaround time for container processing at the facility, measured in hours from gate-in to gate-out. Used for SLA compliance monitoring and performance measurement.
reference: International Association of Ports and Harbors (IAPH) - Service Level Standards

description: State, province, or administrative region where the facility is located. Used for regulatory compliance and regional operational planning.

business_glossary_term: Storage Capacity in Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU)
description: Maximum container storage capacity of the facility measured in TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Units). Used for capacity planning, yard management, and operational load balancing across the intermodal network.
reference: ISO 668 - Container External Dimensions and Ratings

description: Maximum number of trucks that can be accommodated simultaneously in the facility's parking and staging areas. Used for truck appointment system capacity planning and gate congestion management.
reference: International Association of Ports and Harbors (IAPH) - Truck Management Standards

description: Indicates whether the facility operates 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. True for round-the-clock operations; False for facilities with limited operating hours. Impacts truck appointment system configuration and service level agreements.
reference: International Association of Ports and Harbors (IAPH) - Operational Standards

description: Unique identifier for the intermodal transport service. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Each intermodal service (port-to-ICD shuttle, rail corridor) is operated by designated cost center; linking enables service-level cost accumulation for pricing decisions, service rationalization analy

Foreign key linking to tariff.demurrage_schedule. Business justification: Intermodal services define demurrage terms as a core commercial parameter — the demurrage schedule governs free time and escalating rates for containers on the service. Required for service-level cont

Foreign key linking to tariff.detention_schedule. Business justification: Intermodal services include detention terms as a core commercial parameter — the detention schedule defines free time and rates for containers on the service. Required for service-level contract confi

Foreign key linking to intermodal.haulier. Business justification: Truck services are operated by hauliers. The operator_id field is polymorphic (could be rail_operator or haulier depending on transport_mode). When transport_mode = 'TRUCK', this FK links to haulier.

Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal. Business justification: Intermodal services (rail/truck) originate from a specific terminal. Terminal-level service capacity planning, terminal operator commercial agreements with rail/truck operators, and terminal throughpu
Reference to the transport operator or service provider responsible for executing this intermodal service.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Intermodal services operate between defined origin locations. Service planning, capacity allocation, and network design require proper location reference. Replaces denormalized origin_location text fi

Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Intermodal services reference published port tariff schedules to calculate combined transport pricing. Essential for multimodal rate calculation and quote generation in port-hinterland transport opera

Foreign key linking to security.security_zone. Business justification: Scheduled intermodal services operate through designated secure corridors and primary operational zones requiring route security planning, zone-based access control, and MARSEC-level service authoriza

Foreign key linking to intermodal.rail_operator. Business justification: Rail services are operated by rail operators. The operator_id field is polymorphic (could be rail_operator or haulier depending on transport_mode). When transport_mode = 'RAIL', this FK links to rail_

Foreign key linking to tariff.thc_schedule. Business justification: Intermodal services define the transport product offered — the applicable THC schedule is a core commercial parameter of the service. Required for customer quoting, service tariff publication, and THC
Number of hours before scheduled departure that bookings must be submitted. Used for operational planning and customer service commitments.
Maximum container capacity per service departure, measured in TEU. Critical for load planning and service utilization tracking.
Standardized code representing the origin-destination corridor, typically in format XXX-YYY where XXX is origin and YYY is destination location code.
Timestamp when this intermodal service record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Indicates whether this intermodal service includes customs clearance coordination at origin or destination, critical for international cargo movements.
Indicates whether this service is certified and equipped to handle IMDG (International Maritime Dangerous Goods) classified cargo.
End point of the intermodal service corridor. Typically an Inland Container Depot (ICD), rail terminal, or distribution center.

Indicates whether this service supports EDI integration for automated booking, COPARN (Container Pre-Announcement), and IFTMIN (Instruction Message for Transport) messaging with inland logistics partners.
Pipe-separated list of supported EDI message types for this service, such as COPARN, IFTMIN, IFTMBC (Booking Confirmation), IFTSTA (Status Report).
Date when this intermodal service becomes operational and available for booking. Used for service lifecycle management.
reference: Port Management Information System (PMIS) Service Catalog

description: Date when this intermodal service is scheduled to end or be discontinued. Nullable for ongoing services.
reference: Port Management Information System (PMIS) Service Catalog

description: Types of container equipment supported by this service. May include standard dry containers, reefers, open-top, flat-rack, tank containers. Pipe-separated list if multiple types.
reference: IMDG (International Maritime Dangerous Goods Code) Equipment Classification

description: Frequency at which the intermodal service operates. Daily: every day. Weekly: once per week. Bi-weekly: twice per week. On-demand: as requested. Scheduled: fixed timetable.
Number of hours before scheduled departure that containers must be delivered to the origin gate or Container Yard (CY). Critical for load planning.
Timestamp when this intermodal service record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.
Indicates whether this service can accommodate oversize or out-of-gauge cargo such as open-top or flat-rack containers.
Indicates whether this service can handle refrigerated containers with temperature-controlled transport throughout the corridor.
Additional operational notes, restrictions, or special instructions related to this intermodal service. Free-text field for operational teams.
Total distance of the intermodal service route measured in kilometers, used for transit time calculation and tariff determination.
reference: International Organization for Standardization (ISO) Distance Measurement

description: Unique business identifier code for the intermodal service, used in operational systems and customer communications. Typically alphanumeric 6-12 characters.
Detailed description of the intermodal service offering, including route highlights, special features, and operational characteristics. Used for marketing and customer communications.
Human-readable name of the intermodal transport service, used for marketing and operational reference.

Current operational status of the intermodal service. Active: currently operating. Suspended: temporarily halted. Discontinued: permanently ended. Planned: future service. Seasonal: operates during specific periods.

Classification of the intermodal service offering. Rail shuttle: dedicated rail service between port and inland terminal. ICD linkage: connection to Inland Container Depot. Drayage: short-haul truck movement. Inland barge: waterway transport. Road feeder: scheduled truck service. Multimodal corridor: combination of transport modes.
reference: International Association of Ports and Harbors (IAPH) Intermodal Standards

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) On-Time Performance Target
description: Target percentage for on-time delivery performance as defined in customer Service Level Agreements. Measured as percentage of departures/arrivals within committed transit time window.
Benchmark transit time for the service from origin to destination, measured in hours. Used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) commitments and customer expectations.
Primary mode of transport for this service. Rail: railway transport. Truck: road transport. Barge: inland waterway. Multimodal: combination of modes.
reference: IFTMIN (Instruction Message for Transport) EDI Standard

description: Number of scheduled departures per week for this service. Used for capacity planning and customer service commitments.
reference: Port Management Information System (PMIS) Service Catalog

description: Unique identifier for the intermodal slot booking record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card. Business justification: Slot bookings (capacity reservations) are priced using customer-specific rate cards. Critical for booking confirmation, revenue estimation, and capacity management billing in intermodal services.

Foreign key linking to booking.call_booking. Business justification: An intermodal slot booking for container transport must reference the vessel call booking for billing intermodal handling charges and coordinating cargo cut-off times. Finance teams require this link

Foreign key linking to booking.cargo_booking. Business justification: Intermodal slot bookings (rail/truck capacity) coordinate with vessel cargo bookings for transshipment operations, intermodal transfers, and inland container movements. Required for matching container

Foreign key linking to masterdata.commodity_code. Business justification: Slot bookings for dangerous goods require commodity code for pre-notification to port authorities and capacity planning by cargo type. Regulatory pre-clearance and dangerous goods slot allocation depe

Foreign key linking to cargo.container. Business justification: Slot bookings on intermodal services are made for specific containers. Intermodal operators need to know which container occupies which slot for equipment planning, gate coordination, and reefer/hazma

Foreign key linking to masterdata.container_type. Business justification: Slot allocation requires container type to match equipment availability, yard block preference, and handling equipment compatibility. The plain attribute equipment_type_code on slot_booking is a denor

Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_declaration. Business justification: Slot bookings for international cargo require a customs declaration to be lodged before the slot is confirmed. Port community systems verify customs declaration status at slot booking stage — a standa

Foreign key linking to tariff.demurrage_schedule. Business justification: Slot bookings may result in demurrage if containers are not moved within free time. The demurrage schedule at booking level enables proactive customer notification and cost estimation — required for d

Foreign key linking to intermodal.icd_facility. Business justification: A slot booking for intermodal transport specifies a destination location, which in the intermodal context is typically an ICD or CFS facility. slot_booking has destination_location_code as a free-text

Foreign key linking to tariff.detention_schedule. Business justification: Slot bookings for container pickup initiate the detention clock. The detention schedule at booking level defines free time and billing rates — required for detention cost disclosure at booking, custom

Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal_zone. Business justification: Slot bookings for intermodal services specify origin terminal zone for container pickup, enabling capacity reservation, zone allocation planning, and service scheduling - intermodal booking process.
Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, or cargo owner) who made this booking. Links to customer master data.
Reference to the vessel visit if this intermodal booking is for cargo arriving or departing on a specific vessel. Links to vessel operations.

Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening. Business justification: Slot bookings involve cargo owners and operators who must be screened against sanctions lists before booking confirmation. Linking each slot booking to its sanctions screening record is a mandatory AM
Reference to the intermodal service (rail or truck route) for which this slot is booked. Links to the service schedule and capacity plan.

Foreign key linking to cargo.shipment. Business justification: A slot booking on an intermodal service is made for cargo belonging to a specific shipment. Port community systems and intermodal operators link slot bookings to shipments for capacity planning, cargo

Foreign key linking to booking.slot_reservation. Business justification: An intermodal slot booking must be linked to the corresponding vessel slot reservation to confirm end-to-end space allocation from inland origin to vessel. Shipping lines and terminal planners use thi

Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal. Business justification: Slot bookings are made at a specific terminal — terminal capacity planning, slot utilization reporting by terminal, and commercial agreements between shipping lines and terminal operators all require

Foreign key linking to tariff.thc_schedule. Business justification: Slot bookings involve container moves attracting THC. The THC schedule at booking level enables upfront cost disclosure and billing — required for customer invoicing, booking confirmation with cost br

Foreign key linking to intermodal.transport_order. Business justification: Slot bookings reserve intermodal capacity for transport orders. The booking is the capacity reservation; the transport order is the instruction. Linking allows retrieval of AWB and BOL from transport_
Actual date when the intermodal transport service was executed. Used for on-time performance measurement and SLA compliance.
Channel through which the booking was submitted. Used for channel performance analysis and digital adoption tracking.
Date when the slot booking was created or submitted by the customer. Used for booking lead time analysis and service level tracking.
reference: NAVIS N4 Rail Operations - Booking Management

description: Externally-known unique booking reference number issued to the customer for this intermodal slot reservation. Used for customer communication and tracking.
Current lifecycle status of the slot booking. Tracks progression from initial request through fulfillment or cancellation.
reference: NAVIS N4 Rail Operations - Booking Workflow

description: Precise date and time when the booking was created in the system. Used for audit trail and first-come-first-served slot allocation.
Type of intermodal transport mode for this booking. Determines handling procedures and capacity allocation rules.
reference: International Association of Ports and Harbors (IAPH) - Intermodal Standards

description: Standardized code indicating the reason for booking cancellation. Used for root cause analysis and service improvement.
reference: NAVIS N4 Rail Operations - Cancellation Management

description: Date and time when the booking was cancelled. Null if booking is active. Used for cancellation analysis and capacity recovery.
Classification of cargo being transported. Determines handling requirements and equipment allocation for the intermodal service.
Date and time when the booking status changed to confirmed. Used for service level measurement and customer notification tracking.
Actual date confirmed for the intermodal transport slot. Null if booking is pending or waitlisted.
reference: NAVIS N4 Rail Operations - Slot Confirmation

description: Precise date and time window confirmed for the intermodal transport departure or pickup. Used for gate appointment coordination.
Total number of containers included in this slot booking, regardless of size. Used for slot allocation and capacity planning.
reference: NAVIS N4 Rail Operations - Capacity Management

description: System timestamp when this booking record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Customer's own internal reference or purchase order number for this booking. Used for reconciliation and customer reporting.
Reference to the EDI message (IFTMIN, COPARN, or AWB) that initiated or confirmed this booking. Used for EDI reconciliation and audit.
Flag indicating whether this booking includes hazardous materials requiring special handling and compliance documentation.
reference: IMDG Code - International Maritime Dangerous Goods

description: System timestamp when this booking record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.
Flag indicating whether the customer failed to utilize the confirmed slot. Used for no-show penalty assessment and capacity planning.
Flag indicating whether this booking includes oversized or out-of-gauge cargo requiring special equipment or routing.
Payment terms agreed for this booking. Determines billing and collection procedures.
reference: NAVIS N4 Billing Module - Payment Terms

description: Service priority level for this booking. Determines slot allocation preference and service level agreement targets.
Flag indicating whether this booking includes refrigerated containers requiring temperature-controlled transport.
Additional free-text remarks or notes about this booking. Used for operational communication and exception handling.
reference: NAVIS N4 Rail Operations - Booking Management

description: Date requested by the customer for the intermodal transport service. May differ from confirmed slot date based on availability.
Code identifying the operational system that originated this booking record. Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.
Free-text field for special handling requirements, routing instructions, or operational notes for this booking.
reference: NAVIS N4 Rail Operations - Booking Notes

description: Total capacity of this booking expressed in TEU. Calculated from container sizes (20ft=1 TEU, 40ft=2 TEU, 45ft=2.25 TEU). Used for capacity utilization reporting.
Total volume of cargo in this booking expressed in cubic meters. Used for space utilization and capacity planning.
reference: International System of Units (SI) - Volume Measurement

description: Total gross weight of cargo in this booking expressed in metric tonnes. Used for load planning and compliance with weight restrictions.
reference: International System of Units (SI) - Mass Measurement

description: Unique identifier for the rail freight operator. Primary key for the rail operator master record within the intermodal domain.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Rail operator country determines network access agreement jurisdiction, regulatory licensing authority, and sanctions screening requirements. The plain attribute registered_country_code is a denormali

Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_broker. Business justification: Rail operators partner with customs brokers for cross-border freight clearance. Intermodal service providers maintain broker relationships to facilitate customs processing at border crossings and inla

Unique EDI partner identifier used for electronic messaging integration with port systems, enabling automated exchange of IFTMIN (transport instruction), COPARN (container pre-announcement), and other UN/EDIFACT messages.

Foreign key linking to security.facility_security_plan. Business justification: Rail operators interfacing with port terminals must comply with facility security plans for ISPS interface requirements, security procedure alignment, and MARSEC-level coordination - intermodal securi

Foreign key linking to customer.participant_account. Business justification: Rail operators are invoiced through a participant_account for rail terminal handling charges, track usage fees, and wagon handling. Port commercial management and AR processes require a direct billing

Foreign key linking to customer.port_community_participant. Business justification: Rail operators are port community participants requiring accreditation, regulatory compliance (safety/environmental certifications), EDI partner registration, and unified participant registry manageme

Type of container units the operator is equipped to handle, including TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit), FEU (Forty-foot Equivalent Unit), refrigerated containers, and IMDG (International Maritime Dangerous Goods) certified cargo.
Timestamp when this rail operator record was first created in the system.

Credit rating assigned by a recognized rating agency, indicating the financial stability and creditworthiness of the rail operator. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D|not_rated — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Standard & Poor's / Moody's / Fitch Rating Scales

description: Technical protocol used for EDI message exchange with the rail operator (e.g., UN/EDIFACT, XML, REST API, AS2, SFTP).
24/7 emergency contact telephone number for urgent operational issues, incidents, or safety events.
Comma-separated list of environmental certifications held by the rail operator (e.g., ISO 14001, Green Freight certification).
Timestamp when this rail operator record was last updated or modified.
Date on which the regulatory licence expires and must be renewed for continued operations.
reference: National Railway Safety Authority Licensing Framework

description: Comma-separated list of locomotive types operated by the carrier (e.g., diesel, electric, hybrid), indicating traction capability and environmental profile.
reference: International Union of Railways (UIC) Rolling Stock Classification

description: Maximum gross train weight in tonnes that the operator is authorized and equipped to haul, based on locomotive capacity and track load limits.
reference: International Union of Railways (UIC) Technical Standards

description: Maximum train length in meters that the operator can handle, constrained by locomotive power, siding capacity, and network infrastructure.
reference: International Union of Railways (UIC) Technical Standards

description: Reference number or identifier for the network access agreement between the rail operator and the rail infrastructure manager, governing track usage rights and charges.
reference: European Railway Directive 2012/34/EU (or equivalent national framework)

description: Date on which the rail operator was first registered and onboarded into the port's intermodal logistics network.
Current operational status of the rail operator within the port's intermodal network, indicating whether the operator is authorized to provide services.
reference: ISO 22000 - Supply Chain Management Status Codes

description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the rail operator, used in operational systems and EDI messaging (IFTMIN, COPARN). Typically assigned by port authority or national rail registry.
Classification of the rail operator based on service scope and operational model.
Standard payment terms in days for invoices issued to the rail operator for port services (e.g., 30, 60, 90 days).
reference: International Chamber of Commerce (ICC) Payment Terms

description: Composite performance rating score (0.00 to 5.00) based on KPIs such as on-time delivery, cargo handling quality, safety record, and EDI compliance.
Three-letter ISO currency code for the rail operator's preferred invoicing and settlement currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
First line of the registered business address of the rail operator, typically street number and name.
Second line of the registered business address, typically suite, floor, or building name.
City or municipality of the registered business address.
Postal or ZIP code of the registered business address.
State, province, or region of the registered business address.
Official licence or permit number issued by the national railway safety authority authorizing the operator to provide rail freight services.
reference: National Railway Safety Authority Licensing Framework

description: Free-text field for additional notes, special handling instructions, or operational comments related to the rail operator.
Comma-separated list of primary rail corridors or routes served by the operator (e.g., Port-City A, Port-City B-ICD C). Defines geographic service coverage.
Date on which the rail operator's authorization to operate within the port network was terminated or suspended, if applicable.
Official website URL of the rail operator for public information and service inquiries.
Unique identifier for the rail train visit to the port terminal. Primary key for the rail visit record. Represents the rail equivalent of a vessel call — the unit of rail service delivery.

Foreign key linking to booking.call_booking. Business justification: Rail visits are scheduled to coincide with specific vessel call bookings for container interchange planning. Port planners correlate rail arrivals with call bookings for yard capacity, berth schedulin

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Rail terminal operations are cost centers; rail visit costs (track usage, handling, labor) must be captured against responsible cost center for operational cost accounting, variance analysis, and term

Foreign key linking to tariff.demurrage_schedule. Business justification: Demurrage accrues when containers arriving by rail exceed free time at the terminal. The demurrage schedule applicable to a rail visit determines free time days and escalating rates — required for dem

Foreign key linking to cargo.handling_order. Business justification: A rail visit at the port terminal is coordinated with a terminal handling order governing the loading/unloading of containers between rail wagons and the terminal yard. Port planners use this link for

Foreign key linking to compliance.import_export_permit. Business justification: Rail visits transporting controlled or restricted goods require import/export permits. Port and customs authorities verify permit validity before authorising rail departure or arrival, making this a m

Foreign key linking to cargo.cargo_manifest. Business justification: Rail visits must reference the cargo manifest for customs declaration at rail-port interchange, dangerous goods compliance verification, and cargo inventory reconciliation between rail operator and te

Foreign key linking to customer.participant_account. Business justification: Rail visits are billed to a customer participant_account for THC, rail handling charges, and SLA measurement. Port billing systems require a direct account reference on the rail visit to generate invo

Foreign key linking to vessel.call. Business justification: Rail visits are operationally planned against specific vessel calls — port planners coordinate rail arrival/departure windows with vessel berthing schedules to ensure containers discharged from a spec

Foreign key linking to infrastructure.port_gate. Business justification: Rail trains enter/exit through designated port gates. Gate assignment for rail visits is required for ISPS access control compliance, gate throughput KPI reporting, and gate scheduling — a named opera

Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Rail visits occur at specific terminal track locations. Yard planning, resource allocation, and track assignment operations require proper location reference for infrastructure management and operatio

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Rail visits generate port revenue (rail handling charges, THC) that must be attributed to a profit centre for P&L reporting by business unit. Port finance P&L reporting by intermodal rail operations r
Reference to the rail carrier or operator providing the train service. Links to the port community participant master data.

Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal_zone. Business justification: Rail visits operate within dedicated rail-served terminal zones, enabling rail capacity planning, track allocation, and intermodal transfer coordination - rail operations planning process. Role-prefix

Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card. Business justification: Rail visits for contracted operators use negotiated rate cards for terminal services (loading, unloading, track usage). Essential for rail service billing and operator invoicing.

Foreign key linking to intermodal.intermodal_service. Business justification: Rail visits are instances of intermodal rail services. The service defines the scheduled route and frequency; the visit is the actual train arrival/departure. No columns removed because visit has exec

Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal. Business justification: A rail visit occurs at a specific terminal — terminal-level rail throughput reporting, billing of rail handling charges, and terminal capacity planning for rail operations all require rail visits to b

Foreign key linking to tariff.thc_schedule. Business justification: THC is charged per container loaded/discharged during a rail visit. The THC schedule governs terminal handling charges for rail-originated container moves — essential for billing reconciliation and TH

Foreign key linking to security.security_zone. Business justification: Rail operations occur in designated secure rail terminal zones requiring zone-specific access control, hazmat screening protocols, and MARSEC-level security measures - intermodal facility security pla
Actual date and time when the train physically arrived at the port terminal rail facility. Recorded by terminal operations for performance measurement and billing.
Actual date and time when the train departed from the port terminal rail facility. Recorded for performance measurement, billing, and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance.
Reference to the truck appointment system booking if this rail visit is part of an integrated intermodal appointment. Links rail and truck operations.
Current status of billing and invoicing for this rail visit. Tracks the financial lifecycle from charge calculation through payment.

Date and time when the rail visit was marked as completed, indicating all loading/unloading operations finished and the train departed. Used for performance measurement and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance.
Total number of individual containers unloaded from the train during this visit, regardless of size. Used for operational tracking and billing.
Total number of individual containers loaded onto the train during this visit, regardless of size. Used for operational tracking and billing.
Date and time when this rail visit record was first created in the Terminal Operating System (TOS). Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Geographic destination point or Inland Container Depot (ICD) to which the train will proceed after departing the terminal. Used for supply chain visibility and intermodal coordination.
Reference to the EDI IFTMIN (Instruction Message for Transport) or other EDI message that initiated or updated this rail visit. Used for audit trail and system integration.
Number of empty wagons in the train consist. Used for capacity planning and outbound loading operations.
Current estimated arrival time based on real-time tracking and updates from the rail operator. Updated as the train progresses toward the terminal.
Current estimated departure time based on operational progress and remaining work. Updated throughout the visit as operations proceed.

Flag indicating whether the train is carrying any hazardous materials requiring special handling per International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) Code. Triggers safety protocols and regulatory compliance procedures.
reference: IMDG Code - International Maritime Dangerous Goods

description: Date and time when this rail visit record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.
Number of wagons carrying containers or cargo upon arrival. Used for workload estimation and equipment planning.
Rail operator's internal reference or booking number for this train visit. Used for reconciliation and communication with the rail carrier.
Geographic origin point or Inland Container Depot (ICD) from which the train departed. Used for supply chain visibility and intermodal coordination.
Operational priority assigned to the rail visit. Determines sequencing and resource allocation for loading and unloading operations.
Number of refrigerated containers on the train requiring power connection and temperature monitoring. Used for resource planning and power infrastructure allocation.
Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or exceptions related to this rail visit. Used for communication between terminal staff and rail operators.
Planned date and time when the train is scheduled to arrive at the port terminal rail facility. Used for resource planning and berth allocation.
Planned date and time when the train is scheduled to depart from the port terminal rail facility after completing loading/unloading operations.
Type of rail service being provided. Unit train serves single customer/destination, manifest train carries mixed cargo, block train moves pre-assembled groups, local service provides switching.
Total container capacity of the train expressed in Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU). Standard measure for rail capacity planning and performance reporting.
Actual number of TEU unloaded from the train during this visit. Used for productivity measurement and billing calculations.
Actual number of TEU loaded on the train for this visit. Used for productivity measurement and billing calculations.
Identifier of the rail track or siding where the train is positioned for loading and unloading operations. Critical for yard management and equipment dispatch.
Unique identifier assigned to the train by the rail operator. Used for tracking the physical train consist throughout its journey.
Total physical length of the train consist in meters. Used for track capacity planning and safety compliance.
Total weight of the train including wagons and cargo in metric tonnes. Used for infrastructure load management and safety compliance.
Business identifier for the rail visit. Externally-known unique reference number assigned by the Terminal Operating System (TOS) for tracking and communication purposes.

Current lifecycle status of the rail visit. Tracks the visit through scheduling, arrival, operations, and departure phases. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|confirmed|arrived|in_progress|departed|completed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Classification of the rail visit based on operational purpose. Inbound brings containers to port, outbound removes containers, interchange involves both, shuttle provides regular scheduled service.
Total number of rail wagons (railcars) in the train consist. Used for capacity planning and operational resource allocation.
Unique system identifier for the rail wagon record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Rail wagon maintenance, inspection, and operational costs must be attributed to a cost centre for port cost accounting and budget control. Port finance teams report wagon fleet operating costs by cost

Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Rail wagons are capital assets tracked in the fixed asset register for depreciation, insurance, and residual value reporting. rail_wagon already carries acquisition_cost, residual_value, and insurance

description: The maximum cargo weight in kilograms that the rail wagon is certified to carry safely. Critical for load planning and regulatory compliance.

Reference to the specific rail wagon (railcar) that carries this container or cargo unit. Identifies the physical wagon within the rail consist.

reference: IMO Resolution A.960(23); VTMS Operational Procedures; National Maritime Safety Authority Pilotage Regulations

description: Full name of the pilot's designated emergency contact person. Required for safety and incident management procedures under SOLAS and OHS regulations.

description: Phone number of the pilot's designated emergency contact. Used by port operations and marine services teams in the event of an incident or emergency involving the pilot.

description: Relationship of the emergency contact person to the pilot (e.g., spouse, parent, sibling). Provides context for emergency communications.
reference: ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; ILO Maritime Labour Convention 2006

description: Classification of the pilot's employment arrangement with the port authority. Determines applicable HR policies, entitlements, and billing arrangements. Values: permanent, contract, casual, seconded.

description: Assessed level of English language proficiency for maritime communications, as English is the IMO-mandated working language for international pilotage. Values: basic, operational, proficient, expert.

description: Full legal name of the licensed marine pilot as recorded on the pilot licence and identity documents. Used for scheduling, manifests, and regulatory reporting.

business_glossary_term: International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Clearance Level
description: ISPS security clearance level granted to the pilot, determining access permissions to restricted port areas and vessels. Aligned with the ISPS Code security framework. Values: level_1 (normal), level_2 (heightened), level_3 (exceptional).

description: Name of the national maritime authority or regulatory body that issued the pilot licence (e.g., Australian Maritime Safety Authority, Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore). Required for regulatory correspondence and PSC inspections.
reference: IMO Resolution A.960(23); Port State Control (PSC) Authorities

description: Comma-separated list of ISO 639-1 language codes representing languages in which the pilot is proficient for maritime communications. IMO requires English as the working language; additional languages support multi-national vessel operations. Example: 'EN,ZH,AR'.

description: Date of the most recent marine incident or near-miss recorded against this pilot. Used for safety trend analysis and to trigger mandatory review processes under SOLAS and port safety procedures.

description: Date on which the pilot's current licence expires and must be renewed. Used for proactive renewal alerts and to prevent scheduling of pilots with lapsed licences.

description: Date on which the pilot's current medical fitness certificate expires. Triggers renewal workflow and prevents scheduling of pilots with expired medical certificates.

description: Reference number of the pilot's current medical fitness certificate issued by an approved medical examiner. Used for regulatory verification and audit purposes.

description: Current status of the pilot's medical fitness certificate as required under maritime health standards. A valid certificate is a prerequisite for active pilotage duty. Values: valid, expired, suspended, pending_review.

description: Indicates whether the pilot is authorised to conduct pilotage operations during night-time hours. Some pilots may have restrictions based on experience level or port-specific regulations.

description: Date on which the pilot first commenced licensed pilotage operations at this port. Used to calculate seniority, experience tenure, and for workforce planning analytics.

description: Timestamp when the pilot master record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation point for data governance and regulatory compliance purposes.

description: Timestamp when the pilot master record was most recently updated. Supports data lineage tracking, change auditing, and Silver layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.

description: Date by which the pilot must complete the next mandatory refresher or recurrent training programme. Triggers training scheduling workflows and compliance alerts.

description: Free-text field for operational notes, special conditions, or administrative remarks pertaining to the pilot record. May include details on licence restrictions, special authorisations, or operational caveats not captured in structured fields.

description: Reference number of the associated marine incident record when incident_reported is True. Links pilotage assignment to the incident management record for investigation and regulatory reporting.

business_glossary_term: International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Compliance Verified Flag

description: Timestamp when the pilotage assignment record was last modified. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and Silver layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.

business_glossary_term: Minimum Under-Keel Clearance (UKC) Recorded in Metres

description: Total distance of the pilotage passage in nautical miles as recorded upon completion. Used for billing, performance benchmarking, and route validation.

description: Structured narrative of the pilotage passage including key helm orders, manoeuvres, waypoints transited, and significant events. Forms part of the official pilotage completion log for SOLAS compliance and post-incident analysis.

business_glossary_term: Protection and Indemnity (P&I) Club Notified Flag
description: Indicates whether the vessel's Protection and Indemnity (P&I) club has been notified of an incident arising from this pilotage assignment. Required for insurance and liability management.
reference: International Group of P&I Clubs Rules; IMO SOLAS Chapter V; Port Authority Liability Procedures

description: Classification of the pilot's licence indicating the scope of authorisation (e.g., vessel size limits, route restrictions). Ensures the assigned pilot holds the appropriate licence class for the vessel and route.

description: Timestamp when the pilot disembarked the vessel, marking the official end of the pilotage service. Used for billing duration calculation and service completion logging.

description: Timestamp when the pilot assumed navigational advisory responsibility on the bridge, marking the official commencement of the pilotage service. Distinct from boarding time if transfer involved a pilot ladder or boat.

description: Planned date and time at which the pilot is scheduled to board the vessel at the boarding station. Used for pilot dispatch planning and Vessel Traffic Service (VTS) coordination.

description: Classification of the pilotage service indicating the nature of the vessel movement: inbound (arrival), outbound (departure), shifting (berth-to-berth), canal transit, or anchorage movement.

description: Indicates whether tug assistance was required for this pilotage assignment based on vessel characteristics, weather conditions, or port authority requirements.

business_glossary_term: Vessel Traffic Service (VTS) Reporting Point Count
description: Number of mandatory Vessel Traffic Service (VTS) reporting points transited during the pilotage passage. Used for VTS compliance verification and passage plan adherence assessment.
reference: IMO Resolution A.857(20) VTS Guidelines; IALA VTS Standards

Maintained depth of the navigation channel along this route, measured in meters below chart datum. Critical for under-keel clearance calculations.
Width of the navigation channel along this route in meters. Used for safe passing distance and maneuvering calculations.
Indicates whether pilotage is legally compulsory for this route under port regulations. True if mandatory, false if voluntary or exempt vessels exist.

Timestamp when this pilotage route record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.
Maximum vessel beam (width) in meters that can safely navigate this route. Used for channel width restrictions.
Maximum vessel draft in meters that can safely navigate this route under normal tidal conditions. Critical for vessel clearance and safety.
Maximum vessel length overall in meters that can safely navigate this route. Used for turning basin and berth restrictions.

Starting location of the pilotage route, typically a pilot boarding station, anchorage, or berth designation.
Indicates whether vessels are permitted to overtake other vessels on this route. True if overtaking is allowed, false if prohibited due to channel width or safety concerns.
Indicates whether pilot exemption certificates are recognized for this route. True if qualified masters may be granted exemption from compulsory pilotage.

Current operational status of the pilotage route. Active routes are available for scheduling, inactive routes are permanently closed, suspended routes are temporarily unavailable, seasonal routes operate during specific periods.

Human-readable name of the pilotage route, typically including origin and destination points.

Classification of the pilotage route based on operational purpose: inbound (vessel entering port), outbound (vessel departing port), shifting (vessel moving within port), harbor (harbor navigation), or channel (channel transit).
Annual recurring end date for seasonal routes in MM-DD format. Applicable when status is seasonal. Represents the date each year when the route ceases operation.
Annual recurring start date for seasonal routes in MM-DD format. Applicable when status is seasonal. Represents the date each year when the route becomes operational.
Maximum permitted vessel speed through water in knots for this route. Enforced for safety, wake control, or environmental protection.

Indicates whether tug assistance is mandatory for vessels navigating this route. True if towage is required by regulation or port authority directive.
Indicates whether this route includes or intersects with an International Maritime Organization (IMO) Traffic Separation Scheme. True if TSS rules apply.
Indicates whether this route supports simultaneous two-way vessel traffic. True if bidirectional traffic is permitted, false if one-way or alternating traffic only.
Indicates whether this route is within a Vessel Traffic Service area requiring mandatory reporting and communication. True if VTS participation is required.
VHF radio channel number used for Vessel Traffic Service communications along this route. Format: VHF followed by two-digit channel number.
Indicates whether this route has weather-related operational restrictions. True if route is subject to closure or restrictions during adverse weather conditions.

The surveyor's preferred channel for receiving survey dispatch notifications and port communications. Options include email, phone, mobile, pcs_message (Port Community System message), or edi (Electronic Data Interchange).

Timestamp when the surveyor master record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail entry point for the surveyor's lifecycle within the port's data systems, aligned with Silver Layer lineage requirements.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the surveyor master record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and compliance audit trails.
Textual description of the reason for revocation of the surveyor's port authorisation, where applicable. Supports compliance investigations, PSC audit responses, and ISPS security incident records.
reference: ISPS Code; PSC Memoranda of Understanding; National Maritime Safety Authority

description: Detailed description of the specific survey activities, vessel types, cargo categories, or operational areas the surveyor is authorised to cover within port jurisdiction. May reference SOLAS, MARPOL, IMDG, or classification society-specific survey categories.
reference: IMO SOLAS Chapter I Regulation 6; MARPOL Annex I; IACS Unified Requirements

description: Seafarer's identity document or continuous discharge book number, where applicable. Some classification society and P&I surveyors hold seafarer credentials that facilitate vessel boarding under ILO Maritime Labour Convention requirements.

business_glossary_term: Safety of Life at Sea (SOLAS) Certified
description: Indicates whether the surveyor holds a valid certification to conduct surveys under the International Convention for the Safety of Life at Sea (SOLAS). Required for structural, safety equipment, and load line surveys.
reference: IMO SOLAS Chapter I Regulation 6; IACS Unified Requirements

description: Comma-separated list of specialisation codes indicating the surveyor's certified areas of expertise, such as IMDG dangerous goods, MARPOL pollution prevention, SOLAS structural surveys, draught surveys, or reefer cargo. Drives survey assignment matching in the Port Management Information System (PMIS).
reference: IMO SOLAS; MARPOL; IMDG Code; IACS Unified Requirements

description: Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the surveyor by the port authority or accreditation body. Used as the business-facing identifier on survey certificates, gate access systems, and Port Community System (PCS) messaging.
reference: IAPH Port Access Standards; ISPS Code

description: Categorical classification of the surveyor's primary function within port operations. Values: classification (classification society surveyor), pi_correspondent (P&I Club correspondent), cargo (cargo surveyor), condition (condition/on-hire/off-hire surveyor), draught (draught survey specialist).
reference: IAPH; IMO MSC Guidelines; P&I Club Correspondent Standards

description: Comma-separated list of vessel types the surveyor is authorised to survey, such as container vessels, bulk carriers, tankers, RoRo vessels, LNG carriers, or passenger ships. Ensures correct surveyor-to-vessel matching for survey assignments.
reference: IMO SOLAS Chapter I; IACS Unified Requirements; Classification Society Rules

description: Total number of years of professional marine surveying experience. Used for surveyor capability assessment, complex survey assignment eligibility, and SLA performance benchmarking.
reference: IAPH Surveyor Competency Standards; IMO STCW Convention

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each launch boat dispatch event record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the launch_dispatch data product.

Foreign key linking to infrastructure.anchorage_area. Business justification: Launch boats transfer pilots and crew to vessels at anchorage; destination_location and anchorage_block currently text. Essential for pilot boat routing, fuel planning, and service coordination.

Reference to the marine pilot record transported on this dispatch, applicable when dispatch_purpose is 'pilot_transfer'. Enables traceability between pilot assignments and launch dispatch operations.

description: Reference to the associated pilotage order or marine service request that triggered this launch dispatch, enabling traceability between the service request and the physical dispatch execution.

description: Reference to the vessel record that this launch boat dispatch is serving (e.g., the ship receiving the pilot, crew, or surveyor). Links to the vessel master data product.

Actual timestamp when the launch boat departed the jetty. Compared against scheduled departure time to measure on-time performance and SLA compliance.

description: Total weight in kilograms of any cargo or stores carried on this dispatch (e.g., chandler supplies, spare parts, provisions). Must not exceed the launch boat's Safe Working Load (SWL). Null if no cargo carried.
reference: SOLAS Chapter VI; IMO MSC Guidelines; SWL Certification

description: Official maritime authority licence number of the coxswain, verified at dispatch to confirm the operator holds a valid and current certification to operate the launch vessel.

description: Timestamp when this dispatch record was first created in the system, providing the audit trail creation marker for data lineage and compliance purposes.

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the service charge amount (e.g., USD, SGD, AUD). Supports multi-currency port operations and financial reporting.

description: Name or code of the jetty, berth, or landing point from which the launch boat departed for this dispatch mission. Used for operational planning and berth utilisation reporting.
Target location or anchorage position where the launch boat is dispatched to (e.g., anchorage block, berth number, pilot boarding ground coordinates). Supports route planning and safety monitoring.
Name or identifier of the port operations officer or VTS controller who authorised this launch boat dispatch, providing an accountability trail for operational decisions.
reference: ISPS Code; VTMS Standing Orders; ISM Code

description: Operational priority classification of this dispatch. 'routine' = standard scheduled service; 'urgent' = expedited service required; 'emergency' = immediate response (e.g., medical evacuation, safety incident).

description: Primary operational purpose of the launch boat dispatch. Drives resource allocation, safety protocols, and billing tariff selection. reference: IMO FAL Convention; ISPS Code; SOLAS Chapter V

description: Externally-known business reference number assigned to this dispatch event, used for cross-system tracking and communication with vessel agents, pilots, and port operations. Format: LD-YYYY-NNNNNN.
reference: VTMS Pilotage Scheduling Module; IMO FAL Convention

description: Current lifecycle state of the launch boat dispatch event. 'scheduled' = assigned and awaiting departure; 'in_progress' = launch underway; 'completed' = mission accomplished and returned; 'cancelled' = cancelled before departure; 'aborted' = mission terminated mid-operation.
reference: VTMS Pilotage Scheduling Module; ISPS Code

description: Volume of fuel consumed in litres during this dispatch mission, recorded for fleet cost management, GHG emissions tracking, and MARPOL environmental reporting.
reference: MARPOL Annex VI; IMO DCS (Data Collection System); ISO 14001; GHG Protocol

description: Indicates whether a formal marine incident or near-miss report was raised in connection with this dispatch event. True = incident report filed; False = no incident. Triggers P&I club notification workflow when True.
reference: SOLAS Chapter IX ISM Code; MARPOL; P&I Club Notification Requirements; ISO 45001

business_glossary_term: International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Clearance Verified Flag
description: Indicates whether ISPS security clearance was verified for all persons and cargo on this dispatch prior to departure. Mandatory for port facility security compliance.
reference: ISPS Code Part A Section 16; SOLAS Chapter XI-2; IMO

description: Indicates whether all persons on board the launch boat wore life jackets for the duration of this dispatch, as required by SOLAS and port safety standing orders.
reference: SOLAS Chapter III Regulation 20; ISO 45001 OHS; National Maritime Safety Authority

description: Indicates whether MARPOL environmental compliance checks (e.g., no illegal discharge, waste handling) were completed for this dispatch. Mandatory for environmental management system (EMS) audit trails.
reference: MARPOL 73/78 Annexes I-VI; ISO 14001 Environmental Management; IMO

description: Total elapsed time in minutes from actual departure to actual return for this dispatch mission. Stored as a raw operational field to support billing, SLA analysis, and fleet utilisation reporting.
reference: IAPH Port Performance Indicators; Port Tariff Management

description: Indicates whether this dispatch was conducted during night hours (typically between sunset and sunrise as defined by port standing orders). Night operations attract additional safety requirements and may carry premium tariff rates.
reference: SOLAS Chapter V; National Maritime Safety Authority; Port Tariff Schedule

description: Total number of persons (pilots, crew, surveyors, inspectors, chandlers) transported on this dispatch. Must not exceed the launch boat's certified passenger capacity. Critical for SOLAS safety compliance.
reference: SOLAS Chapter III; National Maritime Safety Authority; IMO MSC Circular

business_glossary_term: Protection and Indemnity (P&I) Club Notified Flag
description: Indicates whether the vessel's Protection and Indemnity (P&I) club has been formally notified of an incident arising from this dispatch. Required for insurance and liability management.
reference: P&I Club Rules; IMO ISM Code; SOLAS Chapter IX

description: Name or code of the designated pilot boarding/disembarking ground where the pilot transfer took place (e.g., anchorage block, outer roads, inner harbour). Defined by port authority standing orders.
reference: SOLAS Chapter V Regulation 23; IMO Resolution A.1045(27); VTS

description: Free-text field for the coxswain or dispatcher to record any safety observations, near-misses, equipment anomalies, or unusual conditions encountered during the dispatch mission. Feeds into the port safety management system.
reference: SOLAS Chapter IX ISM Code; ISO 45001 OHS; MARPOL Annex I

description: Planned departure timestamp for the launch boat from the jetty, aligned with the pilotage schedule or service request. Used for SLA monitoring and resource scheduling.
reference: VTMS Pilotage Scheduling Module; IMO FAL Convention

description: Gross charge amount levied for this launch boat dispatch service as per the port tariff schedule. Expressed in the port's operating currency. Used for revenue recognition and billing reconciliation.
reference: Port Tariff Schedule; SAP S/4HANA FI-CO; IAPH Port Pricing Guidelines

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this dispatch record, supporting audit trail requirements and change tracking in the silver layer lakehouse.
reference: ISO 28000 Supply Chain Security; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: Unique identifier for the marine service order. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the shipping agent or port community participant who requested the marine services on behalf of the vessel operator.

Foreign key linking to infrastructure.berth. Business justification: Marine service orders are raised for vessel calls at specific berths, coordinating pilotage, towage, and mooring services. Berth-level service scheduling, resource allocation, and operational readines

Foreign key linking to customer.participant_account. Business justification: Marine service orders generate charges billed to a specific participant_account. The billing/AR process requires this direct link to apply credit limits, post charges, and generate account statements

Foreign key linking to booking.booking_service_order. Business justification: Booking service orders (commercial requests) generate marine service orders (operational execution). Port operations require end-to-end workflow traceability from the booked service request to its mar

Foreign key linking to customer.contact_person. Business justification: Marine service orders require a named operational contact for coordination, notifications, and confirmations (e.g., pilot boarding time alerts, tug readiness notifications). Port operations teams rout

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Service orders are fulfilled by specific operational cost centres. Essential for service costing, profitability analysis by service line, and transfer pricing between port authority business units.
