Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Port finance creates internal orders for project-based marine service cost control and settlement. Major vessel calls (LNG, cruise, emergency) require internal order tracking for capex/opex reporting

Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Marine service orders bundle pilotage, towage, mooring, and launch services for a vessel call. The order is invoiced as a package. Links service order to final invoice for consolidated marine service

Foreign key linking to security.marsec_level_change. Business justification: ISPS requires all marine service orders (pilotage, towage, mooring) to be executed under the prevailing MARSEC level, which dictates additional security measures, screening requirements, and access co

Foreign key linking to tariff.mooring_tariff. Business justification: Marine service orders reference specific mooring tariffs for billing. Currently has tariff_code_mooring as a string. Adding FK to tariff.mooring_tariff enables proper tariff application and rate looku

Foreign key linking to tariff.pilotage_tariff. Business justification: Marine service orders reference specific pilotage tariffs for billing. Currently has tariff_code_pilotage as a string. Adding FK to tariff.pilotage_tariff enables proper tariff application and rate lo

Reference to the licensed pilotage service provider assigned to deliver pilotage services for this order. Must be an authorized pilot organization per port regulations.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Marine service orders generate revenue attributed to profit centres (pilotage, towage, mooring service lines) for P&L reporting. Port finance P&L statements report marine services revenue by profit ce

Foreign key linking to masterdata.service_code. Business justification: Marine service order billing, tariff application, GL account mapping, and revenue recognition require service code master data (billing basis, tax code, tariff schedule) for financial processing and r
Reference to the mooring service provider assigned to deliver line handling services for this order. Must be a certified mooring contractor with trained personnel.
reference: International Labour Organization (ILO) - Maritime Labour Convention

description: Foreign key linking to tariff.towage_tariff. Business justification: Marine service orders reference specific towage tariffs for billing. Currently has tariff_code_towage as a string. Adding FK to tariff.towage_tariff enables proper tariff application and rate lookup.
Reference to the specific vessel call for which marine services are being ordered. Links this service order to the vessel visit event.
Actual date and time when all marine services were completed. Used for final billing calculation and Key Performance Indicator (KPI) measurement.
Actual date and time when marine services commenced. Recorded for billing accuracy, Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance, and operational performance tracking.
Reason for order cancellation if status is cancelled. Examples: vessel schedule change, weather delay, agent request, service provider unavailability.
Date and time when the marine service order was cancelled. Used to calculate cancellation fees per tariff terms and conditions.
Date and time when all marine services in the order were marked as completed and the order was closed. Triggers final billing and invoicing processes.
Date and time when the marine service order was confirmed by the port authority or service coordinator. Marks the transition from requested to confirmed status.
Date and time when the marine service order record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and order lifecycle tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this order. Typically the local port currency unless otherwise agreed with the agent.
Estimated date and time when all requested marine services are expected to be completed. Used for resource planning and scheduling.
Estimated date and time when the marine services are expected to commence. Typically aligned with vessel Estimated Time of Berthing (ETB) or Estimated Time of Departure (ETD).
Estimated total charge for all marine services in this order, calculated from applicable tariff rates and estimated service parameters. Excludes taxes and adjustments.
Indicates whether launch boat services are requested for personnel transfer between shore and vessel, or between vessels.
reference: Safety of Life at Sea (SOLAS) Convention

description: Number of launch boat trips requested for personnel or light cargo transfer. Each round trip between shore and vessel counts as one trip.
Username or identifier of the user who last modified the marine service order record. Tracks accountability for order changes and updates.
Date and time when the marine service order record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.
Number of mooring personnel required for the line handling operation. Typically ranges from 4 to 12 personnel depending on vessel size and berth configuration.
reference: Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) - Marine Operations

description: Indicates whether mooring services (line handling and securing vessel to berth) are requested as part of this service order.
reference: International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Code

description: Number of tug boats requested for the towage operation. Determined by vessel size, weather conditions, and port regulations. Typically ranges from 1 to 4 tugs.
Externally visible unique business identifier for the marine service order, used in communications with agents, service providers, and billing systems. Format: MSO-YYYYNNNN.
Current lifecycle status of the marine service order. Tracks progression from initial request through service delivery completion or cancellation.
Classification of the marine service order based on urgency and scheduling requirements. Emergency orders receive priority dispatch and may incur premium charges.
Designated location where the marine pilot will board or disembark the vessel. Typically specified as a named pilot station, anchorage, or berth identifier.
Indicates whether marine pilotage services are requested as part of this service order. Pilotage is typically mandatory for vessels above certain size thresholds per port regulations.
Specific type of pilotage service requested. Inbound covers entry from sea to berth, outbound covers departure from berth to sea, shifting covers berth-to-berth movements within port.
Priority classification for service delivery scheduling and resource allocation. Critical priority is reserved for safety-related or emergency situations.
Total duration of marine service delivery in minutes, calculated from actual start to actual end. Used for billing time-based charges and productivity analysis.

Free-text field for special handling instructions, safety requirements, or operational notes relevant to the marine service delivery. Examples: dangerous cargo notifications, restricted maneuvering areas, VIP vessel handling.
Indicates whether a marine surveyor boarding is requested for cargo inspection, damage assessment, or compliance verification purposes.

Type of marine survey requested. Cargo surveys verify quantity and condition, hull surveys assess vessel structure, draft surveys measure cargo weight via vessel displacement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cargo|hull|bunker|draft|damage|pre_loading|post_discharge — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Indicates whether towage (tug boat) services are requested as part of this service order. Towage assists with vessel maneuvering, berthing, and unberthing operations.
Minimum total horsepower (HP) required for the towage operation, calculated based on vessel deadweight tonnage (DWT), length overall (LOA), and environmental conditions.
Weather or environmental conditions under which the marine services cannot be safely delivered. Examples: maximum wind speed, minimum visibility, sea state limits.
reference: Safety of Life at Sea (SOLAS) Convention

Reference to the vessel call (port visit) record associated with this mooring operation. A vessel call may have multiple mooring operations (arrival, departure, shifts). Links to the vessel_call data product.

description: Reference to the vessel master record for the vessel receiving the mooring service. Links to the vessel data product for vessel particulars including LOA, DWT, and GRT.

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_master. Business justification: Mooring gang sizing, line specification (SWL), and bollard selection require vessel master data (LOA, beam, DWT) for safe mooring operations and SOLAS compliance. Removes denormalized vessel identifie

Indicates whether this mooring operation is subject to a tariff charge to the vessel operator or shipping agent. Non-billable operations may include emergency services or internal port movements.

description: Number of shore-side bollards engaged during the mooring operation. Used for infrastructure utilisation tracking, SWL compliance, and berth equipment maintenance scheduling in Maximo.

description: Number of breast lines (mooring lines running perpendicular to the vessel's centreline) deployed during the mooring operation. Critical for lateral restraint and SWL compliance verification.

description: Indicates whether shore-side capstans (powered mooring winches) were utilised during this mooring operation to assist in line tensioning and vessel positioning.
reference: OCIMF Mooring Equipment Guidelines (MEG4); Port Equipment Maintenance Standards

description: Gross charge amount levied for this mooring operation in the port's operating currency, as calculated from the applicable tariff code and gang size/duration. Used for revenue recognition and billing reconciliation.

description: Date and time when the mooring operation physically commenced, i.e., when the first line was passed or the unmooring sequence began. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.

description: Date and time when the mooring operation was fully completed, i.e., all lines secured and vessel confirmed fast, or all lines cast off and vessel clear. Used for duration calculation and billing.

description: Date and time when this mooring operation record was first created in the system, representing the audit trail creation point. Used for data lineage and compliance auditing.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the charge amount (e.g., USD, AUD, SGD). Supports multi-currency port operations and financial reporting.

description: Total elapsed time in minutes from commencement to completion of the mooring operation. Captured as a business field from the operational system for KPI reporting and SLA compliance tracking.

description: Number of mooring personnel deployed for this operation. Used for resource planning, billing calculation, and compliance with minimum manning requirements.

description: Name or employee identifier of the supervisor leading the mooring gang for this operation. Used for accountability, incident investigation, and performance management.

description: Number of head lines (forward mooring lines running ahead of the vessel) deployed during the mooring operation. Part of the line configuration record for SWL compliance and operational audit.

description: Free-text description of any mooring irregularity, safety observation, equipment deficiency, or non-conformance observed during the operation. Populated when irregularity_observed is true. Used for incident investigation and safety reporting.

description: Indicates whether any mooring irregularity, safety observation, or non-conformance was recorded during this operation. When true, details are captured in the irregularity_description field.
reference: SOLAS Chapter II-1; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; ISPS Code

description: Date and time when this mooring operation record was most recently modified, supporting audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.

description: Material composition of the mooring lines used in this operation. Relevant for SWL compliance verification and equipment condition assessment. HMPE = High Modulus Polyethylene (e.g., Dyneema).

description: Classification of the physical mooring location type where the vessel is secured: conventional berth alongside a quay, single-point or multi-buoy mooring, dolphin structure, jetty, or open quay face.

description: Externally-known business reference number assigned to this mooring or unmooring service event, used in billing, port management information system (PMIS) records, and inter-agency communications.

description: Current lifecycle state of the mooring operation, tracking progression from planning through execution to completion or cancellation.

description: Classification of the mooring service performed: mooring (securing vessel on arrival), unmooring (releasing vessel on departure), shifting (moving vessel between berths), or re-mooring (re-securing after adjustment).

business_glossary_term: Protection and Indemnity (P&I) Club Notified Flag
description: Indicates whether the vessel's Protection and Indemnity (P&I) Club insurer was notified of an incident or irregularity arising from this mooring operation. Relevant for liability and insurance claim management.

description: Number of spring lines (mooring lines running at an angle fore and aft to prevent surging) deployed during the mooring operation. Part of the full line configuration record.

description: Number of stern lines (aft mooring lines running astern of the vessel) deployed during the mooring operation. Part of the line configuration record for SWL compliance and operational audit.
reference: OCIMF Mooring Equipment Guidelines (MEG4); SOLAS Chapter II-1

description: Indicates whether all mooring lines and equipment used in this operation were verified to be within their Safe Working Load (SWL) limits at the time of the operation. Mandatory compliance check per OCIMF and port safety regulations.
reference: OCIMF Mooring Equipment Guidelines (MEG4); ISO 45001; SOLAS Chapter II-1

description: Total number of mooring lines deployed across all configurations (head, stern, breast, spring) for this operation. Used for equipment inventory reconciliation and billing.

Indicates whether tug assistance was used in conjunction with this mooring operation to manoeuvre the vessel to or from the berth. Used for service coordination and billing linkage to towage operations.
reference: IAPH Port Operations Standards; IMO Resolution A.1045(27)

description: Indicates the vessel movement context for which the mooring service is being performed: arrival at berth, departure from berth, or an intra-port shift between berths or positions.
reference: VTMS Vessel Movement Classification; IMO FAL Convention

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each towage service order record. Primary key for the towage_order data product.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System; IMO Resolution A.994(25) Guidelines on Towage

description: Reference to the shipping agent or vessel operator who requested the towage service. Primary party reference linking the towage order to the commercial counterparty responsible for the vessel.
reference: Port Community System (PCS); IMO FAL Convention Form 1

description: Foreign key linking to infrastructure.anchorage_area. Business justification: Tugs service vessels at anchorage for shifting, emergency response, and pre-berthing preparation. Common maritime operation requiring anchorage identification for dispatch and billing.
Reference to the berth or terminal location that is the origin or destination of the towage movement. Used for berth allocation planning and towage route coordination.
reference: NAVIS N4 Berth Planning Module; TOPS Expert TOS

description: Foreign key linking to customer.participant_account. Business justification: Towage charges are invoiced against a specific participant_account (not just the participant entity). Accounts receivable and billing teams require this direct link to post towage charges to the corre

Foreign key linking to masterdata.cargo_category. Business justification: Hazmat towage risk assessment and special instructions require cargo category classification. Tug masters and port operations must know cargo category for IMDG/MARPOL compliance during towage; imdg_ha

Foreign key linking to infrastructure.channel. Business justification: Tugs escort vessels through channels; towage planning requires channel depth, width, tidal restrictions, and navigational aid coordination. Critical for safe vessel movements and resource allocation.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Towage services are delivered by specific operational cost centres (marine services, harbour operations). Required for activity-based costing, budget tracking, and operational cost allocation in port

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Emergency salvage towage and major dry-dock moves are tracked against finance internal orders for project cost settlement and insurance recovery. Port finance teams require internal order linkage to s
Reference to the billing invoice record generated for this towage service. Links to the financial invoice data product in the Finance domain for accounts receivable tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Financial Accounting; Port Billing Tariff Schedule

description: Foreign key linking to marine.marine_service_order. Business justification: Towage order is the operational execution of towage services ordered via marine_service_order. The marine_service_order product has towage_required flag, number_of_tugs, and tug_horsepower_required, i
Reference to the marine pilot assigned to the vessel during the towage operation. Towage and pilotage are coordinated services; the pilot directs tug movements during berthing/unberthing.
reference: VTMS Pilotage Scheduling Module; STCW Convention; IMO Resolution A.960(23)

description: FK to vessel.call.call_id — Marine service billing and operational coordination require linking towage orders to the vessel call being serviced. Without this, towage cannot be reconciled to vessel visits.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Towage orders generate revenue attributed to the towage services profit centre for P&L reporting. Port finance management accounts report towage revenue and margin by profit centre — essential for com

business_glossary_term: Requesting Agent Port Community Participant Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to customer.port_community_participant. Business justification: Towage orders are raised by shipping agents who are port community participants. Direct link supports agent authorization checks, credit limit validation, billing account lookup, and service request t

Foreign key linking to tariff.towage_tariff. Business justification: Towage billing process: each towage_order must reference the applicable towage_tariff to calculate charges, validate billing amounts, and support invoice reconciliation. Maritime-logistics billing exp

Foreign key linking to booking.call_booking. Business justification: Towage orders are raised from vessel call bookings specifying tug requirements. Booking drives tug resource allocation, scheduling, and billing. Core operational link between booking and towage servic
Reference to the vessel requiring towage assistance. Links to the vessel master data product for full vessel details.
reference: IMO Ship Identification Number Scheme; SOLAS Chapter XI-1 Reg 3

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_master. Business justification: Towage planning and tug assignment calculations require vessel master data (LOA, beam, DWT, GRT) for bollard pull determination, tariff calculation, and P&I club verification. Removes denormalized ves

Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: Towage tariff calculation, tug count determination, and bollard pull requirements depend on vessel type classification (container, tanker, bulk carrier) for service specification and pricing. Vessel t

Foreign key linking to marine.weather_tide_window. Business justification: towage_order stores wind_speed_knots, wind_direction, and visibility_category inline. These operational conditions are authoritatively captured in weather_tide_window. Adding this FK normalises towage

Free-text description of the reason for aborting or cancelling the towage operation. Mandatory when service_outcome is 'aborted' or 'cancelled'. Used for root cause analysis and operational improvement.
reference: SOLAS Chapter V; Port Incident Reporting Procedures

description: Actual date and time when the towage operation commenced, defined as first tug line made fast to the vessel. Used for KPI measurement, billing, and SLA compliance reporting.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System; Port Billing Tariff Schedule

description: Actual date and time when the towage operation was completed, defined as last tug line cast off from the vessel. Used for duration calculation, billing, and KPI reporting.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System; Port Billing Tariff Schedule

description: Current billing and invoicing status of the towage order. 'pending' = charge calculated but not yet invoiced; 'invoiced' = invoice raised; 'disputed' = charge under dispute; 'paid' = payment received; 'waived' = charge waived by port authority.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Financial Accounting; Port Billing Procedures

description: System timestamp when the towage order record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the towage charge amount (e.g., USD, EUR, SGD, AUD). Required for multi-currency port operations and financial reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Financial Accounting

description: Recorded tidal current speed in knots at the berth or channel location at the time of towage. Critical environmental parameter influencing tug power requirements and safety assessment.
reference: IHO Hydrographic Standards; PIANC Guidelines for Towage; SOLAS Chapter V

description: Total duration of the towage operation in minutes, calculated from actual_commencement to actual_completion. Used for billing, performance benchmarking, and port throughput analytics.
reference: Port Billing Tariff Schedule; IAPH Port Performance Indicators

business_glossary_term: International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) Hazardous Material Flag
description: Indicates whether the vessel is carrying International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) classified hazardous cargo at the time of towage. True = hazardous cargo on board. Influences tug safety protocols and emergency response readiness.
reference: IMDG Code (IMO); SOLAS Chapter VII; MARPOL Annex III

description: Minimum total bollard pull force in metric tonnes required for the towage operation, calculated based on vessel DWT, environmental conditions, and port-specific towage assessment criteria.
reference: PIANC Guidelines for Towage; IMO MSC Circular 1331; Bureau Veritas Tug Standards

description: Externally-known human-readable reference number assigned to the towage order, used in communications with shipping agents, tug operators, and port authority. Format: TOW-YYYY-NNNNNN.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System; Port Community System (PCS)

description: Current lifecycle status of the towage order. 'requested' = initial request received; 'confirmed' = tugs allocated and confirmed; 'in_progress' = towage operation underway; 'completed' = service successfully delivered; 'aborted' = commenced but terminated early; 'cancelled' = cancelled before commencement.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System; IMO Resolution A.994(25)

business_glossary_term: United Nations Location Code (UN/LOCODE) Port Code
description: UN/LOCODE five-character port code identifying the port where the towage service is performed. Supports multi-port operations and regulatory reporting to IMO and national maritime authorities.
reference: UN/LOCODE Standard (UNECE Recommendation 16); IMO FAL Convention

description: Date and time when the towage service was formally requested by the shipping agent or vessel operator. Principal business event timestamp marking the start of the towage order lifecycle.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System; IMO FAL Convention

description: Indicates whether a safety observation, near-miss, or safety concern was recorded during the towage operation. True = safety observation exists. Triggers mandatory safety review process per SOLAS and ISPS requirements.
reference: SOLAS Chapter V; ISPS Code; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety

description: Free-text description of any safety observations, near-misses, or hazards identified during the towage operation. Populated when safety_observation_flag is True. Used for safety management system reporting.
reference: SOLAS Chapter V; ISPS Code; ISO 45001; MARPOL Annex I

description: Planned date and time for the towage operation to commence, aligned with the vessel's Estimated Time of Berthing (ETB) or Estimated Time of Departure (ETD) schedule.
reference: VTMS Vessel Traffic Management System; IMO FAL Convention

description: Final outcome of the towage service delivery. 'completed' = full service delivered successfully; 'aborted' = commenced but terminated before completion; 'cancelled' = cancelled before commencement; 'partial' = service partially delivered due to operational constraints.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System; Port SLA Framework

description: Standard Carrier Alpha Code (SCAC) of the shipping line or vessel operator on whose behalf the towage is requested. Four-letter uppercase code per NMFTA standard.
reference: NMFTA SCAC Code Standard; Port Community System (PCS)

description: Free-text field for any special operational instructions, constraints, or requirements for the towage operation, such as restricted manoeuvring areas, hazardous cargo considerations, or vessel-specific handling notes.
reference: SOLAS Chapter V; IMDG Code; Port Operations Manual

description: Gross towage service charge amount in the port's base currency, calculated per the applicable port tariff schedule based on vessel GRT, towage type, duration, and number of tugs. Used for billing and revenue reporting.
reference: Port Tariff Schedule; SAP S/4HANA FI Billing; IAPH Port Tariff Guidelines

description: Classification of the towage operation by purpose. 'arrival' = vessel inbound to berth; 'departure' = vessel outbound from berth; 'shifting' = vessel moving between berths within port; 'emergency' = unplanned emergency towage; 'dry_dock' = towage to/from dry dock facility.
reference: PIANC Guidelines for Towage; IMO Resolution A.994(25)

description: Indicates whether a tug is required to be attached at the bow (forward) of the vessel as part of the towage configuration. True = bow tug required.
reference: PIANC Guidelines for Towage; Port Towage Procedures Manual

description: Indicates whether a tug is required to be attached breast (alongside) the vessel as part of the towage configuration. True = breast tug required.
reference: PIANC Guidelines for Towage; Port Towage Procedures Manual

description: Indicates whether a tug is required to be attached at the stern (aft) of the vessel as part of the towage configuration. True = stern tug required.
reference: PIANC Guidelines for Towage; Port Towage Procedures Manual

description: Actual number of tug vessels assigned and dispatched for the towage operation. May differ from tugs_required if operational adjustments are made.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System; PIANC Guidelines for Towage

description: Total number of tug vessels required to safely execute the towage operation as determined by the port's towage assessment based on vessel LOA, DWT, wind, and current conditions.
reference: PIANC Guidelines for Towage; Port Towage Regulations

description: System timestamp when the towage order record was last modified. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance.
Unique surrogate identifier for each tug vessel record in the port's marine services master data. Primary key for the tug asset identity table. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE.
reference: IMO Ship Identification Number Scheme; ISO 28000 Supply Chain Security

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Tugs are capital assets requiring depreciation, maintenance capex tracking, insurance valuation, and asset register compliance. Core fixed asset management requirement for maritime fleet financial rep

Foreign key linking to masterdata.flag_state. Business justification: Tug regulatory compliance, PSC inspection targeting, and flag state survey requirements depend on flag state master data (PSC targeting factor, flag state authority, MOU membership) for operational co

Foreign key linking to infrastructure.berth. Business justification: Tugs are stationed at specific tug berths for dispatch readiness. Tug mobilisation time calculations, berth allocation planning, and port asset management all require knowing each tug's home berth. Cr

Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Tug fleet deployment planning and dry-dock scheduling requires linking tugs to their home port. Port operations managers allocate tugs by home port; home_port is a denormalized string that should refe

business_glossary_term: Operating Company Port Community Participant Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to customer.port_community_participant. Business justification: Tug operating companies are licensed port_community_participants. Linking tug to its operating company enables contract management, ISPS compliance verification, insurance tracking, and regulatory rep

Foreign key linking to infrastructure.port. Business justification: Tugs are licensed and operated within a specific port. home_port is a denormalized text field. Port-level tug fleet management, regulatory licensing, and towage service availability reporting require

Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_master. Business justification: Tugs are vessels requiring vessel master data (classification society, P&I club, flag state authority, PSC inspection history) for regulatory compliance, insurance verification, and operational status

Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: Tug classification (harbor tug, ocean-going tug, AHTS, escort tug) requires vessel type master data for capability categorization, tariff classification, and service assignment matching. Tug_type fiel

business_glossary_term: Anchor Handling and Towing Supply (AHTS) Capable Flag
description: Indicates whether the tug vessel is equipped and certified for anchor handling and towing supply (AHTS) operations, including offshore anchor deployment and retrieval. True = AHTS capable; False = harbour towage only. Relevant for ports serving offshore oil and gas support operations.
reference: Classification Society AHTS Notation Rules; IMO Guidelines for Offshore Supply Vessels

description: Class of the Automatic Identification System (AIS) transponder fitted on the tug vessel. Class A transponders are mandatory for SOLAS vessels and provide full dynamic data; Class B transponders are used on smaller non-SOLAS vessels with reduced transmission frequency. Determines the vessel's real-time tracking capability within the port's VTS system.
reference: SOLAS Chapter V Regulation 19; ITU-R M.1371; IMO Resolution MSC.74(69); VTS Operational Procedures

description: Maximum breadth (beam) of the tug vessel in metres measured at the widest point of the hull. Used in navigation clearance calculations, dry dock planning, and assessing compatibility with port infrastructure such as lock chambers and fendering systems.
reference: IMO Ship Measurement Convention; PIANC Harbour Design Standards

description: Certified static bollard pull force of the tug in metric tonnes, as measured and certified under controlled test conditions. The principal performance metric used to match tug capability to vessel assistance requirements based on the assisted vessel's DWT, LOA, and environmental conditions. Critical for berth allocation planning and safe towage operations.
reference: IMO MSC/Circ.884 Tug Bollard Pull Certification; PIANC Working Group Reports; ISO 3828 Bollard Pull Test

description: Name of the shipyard where the tug vessel was constructed. Retained for asset provenance records, warranty claims, technical documentation retrieval, and historical maintenance reference in Maximo Asset Management.
reference: Maximo Asset Management Asset Record; IMO Ship Construction Certificate

description: International radio call sign assigned to the tug vessel by the flag state telecommunications authority. Used for VHF radio communications with Vessel Traffic Service (VTS) and port control during towage and escort operations.
reference: ITU Radio Regulations; IMO SOLAS Chapter IV; VTS Operational Procedures

description: Full classification notation string assigned by the classification society, describing the vessel's structural class, machinery class, and any additional notations (e.g., fire-fighting, escort, ice class). Provides a concise technical summary of the vessel's certified capabilities and construction standards.
reference: IACS Unified Requirements; Classification Society Rules for Tugs and Workboats

description: Date by which the next mandatory classification society survey (annual, intermediate, or special/renewal) must be completed to maintain the vessel's class certificate in good standing. Used in maintenance planning and operational availability forecasting in Maximo Asset Management.
reference: IACS Survey Requirements; Classification Society Survey Schedules; IMO SOLAS Chapter I

description: Name of the international classification society (e.g., Lloyd's Register, DNV, Bureau Veritas, ABS, ClassNK) that has classed the tug vessel and issues its class certificates. Determines the survey regime, structural standards, and equipment certification applicable to the vessel.
reference: IACS (International Association of Classification Societies) Member Rules; IMO SOLAS Chapter II

description: Date on which the tug vessel was formally commissioned into service at the port, either as a new build delivery or upon commencement of a charter/contract arrangement. Marks the start of the vessel's operational lifecycle record in Maximo Asset Management and the port's marine services SSOT.
reference: Maximo Asset Management Asset Commissioning; IMO Ship Delivery Certificate; SAP S/4HANA PM Asset Master

description: Date on which the current charter or service contract for the tug vessel is scheduled to expire. Nullable for open-ended arrangements. Used to trigger contract renewal workflows and ensure continuity of towage service capacity planning.
reference: BIMCO Charter Party Terms; SAP S/4HANA FI Contract Management

description: Date on which the current charter or service contract for the tug vessel became effective. Applicable for chartered or contracted tugs. Used in commercial management, billing cycle determination, and contract renewal planning in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: BIMCO Charter Party Terms; SAP S/4HANA FI Contract Management

description: Minimum number of crew members required to safely operate the tug vessel as specified in the vessel's Safe Manning Certificate issued by the flag state. Used in workforce planning, shift rostering, and compliance with ILO Maritime Labour Convention (MLC 2006) crewing requirements.
reference: IMO Safe Manning Certificate (SOLAS Chapter V Regulation 14); ILO Maritime Labour Convention (MLC 2006); National Maritime Safety Authority

description: Date on which the tug vessel was formally withdrawn from service at the port, either permanently or for an extended lay-up period. Nullable for vessels currently in service. Used to close the asset lifecycle record and trigger final compliance and financial close-out processes.
reference: Maximo Asset Management Asset Retirement; SAP S/4HANA PM Asset Lifecycle; IMO Ship Recycling Convention

description: Maximum operational draught of the tug vessel in metres. Used to assess navigability in shallow harbour areas, tidal windows for operations, and compliance with port water depth restrictions. Critical for safe operations in ports with tidal or dredging constraints.
reference: IMO Load Line Convention; IHO Hydrographic Survey Standards; PIANC Dredging Guidelines

description: Total installed main engine power output of the tug vessel measured in kilowatts (kW). Used alongside bollard pull rating to assess tug capability, fuel consumption planning, and compliance with port emission standards. Sourced from the vessel's engine certificate and classification society records.
reference: IMO MARPOL Annex VI NOx Technical Code; Classification Society Engine Certification; ISO 3046 Reciprocating Internal Combustion Engines

description: Certified escort bollard pull force in metric tonnes, measured under dynamic escort conditions as distinct from static bollard pull. Applicable only to escort-certified tugs. Used to determine the maximum DWT of tankers and gas carriers the tug can safely escort through the port approach channel.
reference: IACS Unified Requirement W31; IMO MSC Guidelines on Escort Tug Operations

description: Indicates whether the tug vessel holds a valid escort tug certification from its classification society, confirming it meets the structural, stability, and equipment standards required for escort towage of large tankers and gas carriers in restricted waterways. True = certified for escort operations; False = not certified.
reference: IACS Unified Requirement W31 Escort Tug; IMO MSC Guidelines on Escort Tug Operations; PIANC Escort Tug Standards

description: IMO/classification society fire-fighting system class notation of the tug vessel. FiFi1 denotes basic fire-fighting capability; FiFi1+WS includes water spray; FiFi2 and FiFi3 denote progressively higher capacity systems. 'none' indicates no certified fire-fighting equipment. Determines eligibility for fire-fighting standby duties and emergency response assignments in the port.
reference: IMO MSC/Circ.1239 Fire-Fighting Systems; SOLAS Chapter II-2; Classification Society FiFi Notation Rules

description: Primary fuel type used by the tug vessel's main engines. HFO = Heavy Fuel Oil; MGO = Marine Gas Oil; MDO = Marine Diesel Oil; LNG = Liquefied Natural Gas; methanol = methanol fuel; hybrid = dual-fuel or battery-hybrid arrangement. Used in emissions reporting under MARPOL Annex VI, GHG tracking, and port environmental management.
reference: MARPOL Annex VI; IMO MEPC GHG Strategy; Environmental Monitoring System (EMS); ISO 14001

description: Gross Registered Tonnage (GRT) of the tug vessel as certified under the International Convention on Tonnage Measurement of Ships. Used for port dues calculation, PSC inspection scheduling, and regulatory reporting to maritime authorities.
reference: IMO Tonnage Convention 1969; IAPH Port Dues Tariff Framework

description: Ice class notation assigned by the classification society indicating the tug vessel's structural capability to operate in ice-covered waters. Ranges from 'IA Super' (highest) to 'ID' (lowest ice class) or 'none' for no ice capability. Relevant for ports in seasonal ice regions and for compliance with IACS Polar Class requirements.
reference: IACS Unified Requirements for Polar Class Ships; Finnish-Swedish Ice Class Rules; IMO Polar Code

business_glossary_term: International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Certificate Expiry Date
description: Expiry date of the International Ship Security Certificate (ISSC) issued under the ISPS Code. Confirms the vessel has an approved Ship Security Plan and complies with SOLAS Chapter XI-2 security requirements. Required for port access and monitored by port facility security officers.
reference: ISPS Code (SOLAS Chapter XI-2); IMO MSC/Circ.1192; National Maritime Security Authority

description: Date on which the tug vessel most recently completed a dry docking for hull inspection, underwater maintenance, and classification society survey. Used in maintenance planning to calculate the next scheduled dry dock and assess hull condition for operational readiness.
reference: Classification Society Dry Dock Survey Requirements; Maximo Asset Management Preventive Maintenance; IACS Survey Guidelines

description: Length Overall (LOA) of the tug vessel in metres, measured from the foremost point to the aftermost point of the hull. Used in berth planning, harbour navigation clearance assessments, and determining operational constraints within the port basin.
reference: IMO Ship Measurement Convention; PIANC Harbour Design Standards; IHO Hydrographic Survey Standards

description: Expiry date of the vessel's MARPOL compliance certificate (International Oil Pollution Prevention Certificate or equivalent), confirming the tug meets IMO Marine Pollution Convention requirements for prevention of pollution from ships. Monitored for environmental compliance and PSC inspection readiness.
reference: MARPOL 73/78 Annex I; IMO MEPC Resolutions; Environmental Monitoring System (EMS)

description: Maximum service speed of the tug vessel in knots under normal operating conditions. Used in marine service scheduling to calculate transit times to vessel rendezvous positions, optimise tug dispatch timing, and assess response capability for emergency towage.
reference: Classification Society Sea Trial Records; IAPH Tug Performance Standards

description: Nine-digit Maritime Mobile Service Identity number used for Automatic Identification System (AIS) transmissions and Digital Selective Calling (DSC) communications. Enables real-time vessel tracking via VTS and port monitoring systems.
reference: ITU-R M.585; IMO Resolution MSC.74(69) Annex 3; SOLAS Chapter V Regulation 19

description: Net Registered Tonnage (NRT) of the tug vessel as certified under the International Convention on Tonnage Measurement of Ships. Represents the vessel's earning capacity and is used in certain port tariff calculations and regulatory filings.
reference: IMO Tonnage Convention 1969; IAPH Port Dues Tariff Framework

description: Planned date for the tug vessel's next dry docking. Used in long-term maintenance scheduling, operational availability planning, and budget forecasting for CAPEX dry dock expenditure in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: Classification Society Survey Schedules; Maximo Asset Management Preventive Maintenance Planning; SAP S/4HANA PM

description: National official registration number assigned by the flag state maritime authority at the port of registry. Complements the IMO number as a domestic regulatory identifier used in national maritime administration and port authority records.
reference: National Maritime Safety Authority Registration Regulations; Flag State Ship Registration Act

description: Current lifecycle and availability status of the tug vessel within port marine services operations. 'available' indicates ready for dispatch; 'assigned' indicates currently engaged on a towage or escort job; 'maintenance' indicates undergoing scheduled or unscheduled maintenance; 'out_of_service' indicates temporarily non-operational; 'laid_up' indicates long-term decommissioning or storage.
reference: IAPH Port Operations Guidelines; Maximo Asset Management Lifecycle States; IMO ISM Code

description: Classification of the commercial arrangement under which the port or towage operator controls the tug vessel. 'owned' = port authority or operator owns the vessel outright; 'bareboat_charter' = vessel chartered without crew; 'time_charter' = vessel chartered with crew for a fixed period; 'contracted' = third-party towage company provides the vessel under a service contract.
reference: BIMCO Standard Charter Party Forms; SAP S/4HANA FI Contract Management; IAPH Commercial Guidelines

description: Legal name of the company that holds registered ownership of the tug vessel. May differ from the operating company in cases of bareboat charter or leasing arrangements. Used in commercial contracts, insurance documentation, P&I Club notifications, and port authority vessel registration records.
reference: IMO Ship Registration; P&I Club Membership Rules; SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master

description: Name of the Protection and Indemnity (P&I) Club providing third-party liability insurance coverage for the tug vessel. P&I insurance covers liabilities arising from towage operations including collision, pollution, and crew injury. Required for port entry and towage service authorisation under ISPS and port authority regulations.
reference: International Group of P&I Clubs Rules; IMO SOLAS; IAPH Port Entry Requirements

description: Date on which the current P&I Club insurance certificate expires. Used to trigger renewal alerts and ensure continuous insurance coverage. Tugs with expired P&I cover must not be dispatched for towage operations under port authority rules.
reference: International Group of P&I Clubs Rules; IAPH Port Entry Requirements; IMO ISM Code

description: Policy or certificate number of the current P&I Club insurance cover for the tug vessel. Used in incident reporting, claims management, and port authority compliance verification. Required to be presented during PSC inspections and port entry clearance.
reference: International Group of P&I Clubs Certificate of Entry; IMO ISM Code; ISPS Code

description: Name of the port where the tug vessel is officially registered as recorded on the vessel's certificate of registry. Displayed on the vessel's stern and used in official maritime documentation and PSC inspections.
reference: IMO Ship Registration Standards; UNCLOS Article 91

description: Free-text field for recording operational notes, special conditions, known limitations, or temporary restrictions applicable to the tug vessel that are not captured in structured fields. Examples include temporary equipment deficiencies, operational restrictions imposed by the classification society, or special port authority conditions.
reference: IAPH Port Operations Guidelines; IMO ISM Code Deficiency Reporting

description: Expiry date of the Safety Management Certificate (SMC) issued under the ISM Code, confirming the vessel's safety management system complies with IMO requirements. Tugs operating under the ISM Code must maintain a valid SMC. Used in compliance monitoring and PSC inspection preparation.
reference: IMO ISM Code (Resolution A.741(18)); SOLAS Chapter IX; PSC Inspection Procedures

description: Official registered name of the tug vessel as recorded on the vessel's certificate of registry. Used as the primary human-readable identity label in port operations, VTS communications, and marine service scheduling.
reference: IMO Ship Registration Standards; IAPH Port Operations Guidelines

description: Classification of the tug vessel by propulsion and manoeuvring system design. ASD (Azimuth Stern Drive) tugs offer 360-degree thrust; conventional tugs use fixed propellers; voith tugs use Voith-Schneider cycloidal propellers; tractor tugs use forward-mounted azimuth drives; rotor tugs use a hybrid arrangement. Determines operational capability and assignment suitability for specific towage tasks.
reference: IAPH Tug Classification Standards; IMO MSC Circular on Tug Operations

description: Calendar year in which the tug vessel was constructed and delivered from the shipyard. Used in asset lifecycle management, maintenance planning, insurance valuation, and assessing compliance with age-related regulatory requirements under PSC and flag state rules.
reference: IMO Ship Construction Standards; Maximo Asset Management Lifecycle; Classification Society Rules

description: Unique identifier for the tug assignment record. Primary key for the tug assignment entity.

Foreign key linking to infrastructure.berth. Business justification: Tug assignments are executed at specific berths when assisting vessel berthing/unberthing. Berth-level tug utilisation reporting, towage billing reconciliation, and berth operational planning all requ

Foreign key linking to infrastructure.channel. Business justification: Escort towage assignments occur within specific channels. Channel-level tug escort requirements (mandated by port directions for certain vessel sizes/types) and channel traffic management reporting re

Foreign key linking to billing.charge_event. Business justification: Individual tug assignments generate charge events (per-tug billing based on bollard pull, duration, and engagement time). Each assignment creates a billable event. Links tug assignment to charge event

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Individual tug deployments are cost-tracked for fuel, crew, and maintenance allocation to operational cost centres. Required for activity-based costing and tug fleet profitability analysis in port ope

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Individual tug assignments for emergency response or project-based deployments are settled against internal orders for cost attribution. Port finance requires tug assignment costs to be traceable to s
Self-referencing FK on tug_assignment (reassigned_tug_assignment_id)

Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: Tug operations in restricted waters trigger security incidents when tugs enter unauthorized security zones, violate MARSEC-level movement restrictions, or experience security breaches during vessel es
Reference to the parent towage order that this tug assignment fulfills. Links the individual tug assignment to the overall towage service request.
Reference to the specific tug vessel assigned to this towage operation. Identifies which tug from the fleet is performing the service.

Foreign key linking to marine.weather_tide_window. Business justification: tug_assignment stores wind_speed_knots, wind_direction_degrees, visibility_nm, sea_state_code, tide_height_m, and weather_conditions inline. weather_tide_window is the SSOT for all these operational c
Detailed explanation if the tug assignment was aborted before completion. Captures operational, safety, or environmental factors that prevented completion.
Actual date and time when the tug returned to its berth or anchorage after completing the towage assignment. Used for utilisation tracking and billing.
Actual date and time when the tug departed from its berth or anchorage to commence the towage assignment. Used for performance tracking and billing.

The designated attachment position or role of the tug relative to the vessel being assisted. Critical for operational coordination and safety planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bow|stern|port_bow|starboard_bow|port_quarter|starboard_quarter|standby|escort — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Total duration of the tug assignment from mobilisation to demobilisation, measured in minutes. Used for billing and performance analysis.
Business reference number for this tug assignment, used for operational tracking and communication. May follow port-specific numbering conventions.
Final outcome of the tug assignment. Indicates whether the towage operation was completed successfully or encountered issues.

Current lifecycle status of the tug assignment. Tracks the progression from scheduling through completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|mobilising|on_station|engaged|demobilising|completed|cancelled|aborted — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Indicates whether this tug assignment is billable to the customer. Some assignments may be non-billable due to operational reasons or contractual arrangements.
Reference number linking this tug assignment to the billing system. Used for revenue recognition and invoice reconciliation.
Actual bollard pull force applied by the tug during the towage operation, measured in tonnes. Critical for safety assessment and performance verification.

description: Maximum horizontal reach or span of the equipment in metres. Applicable to cranes (STS, QC, MHC, RTG, ASC) and indicates the operational envelope for cargo handling. Used for berth planning and yard layout optimization.
reference: International Association of Ports and Harbors (IAPH) - Terminal Equipment Specifications

description: Date of the next planned preventive maintenance activity for the equipment. Used for maintenance scheduling, resource planning, and operational availability forecasting.

reference: ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: Current operational lifecycle state of the terminal handling equipment. description: Type or level of operator certification required to operate this equipment. References specific training programs, licensing requirements, or competency standards per port safety regulations and Maritime Labour Convention.

description: Legal ownership or control arrangement for the equipment. Owned indicates port authority owns the asset, leased indicates long-term lease arrangement, rented indicates short-term rental, contractor_provided indicates equipment supplied by third-party service contractor.

description: Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, operational constraints, or historical information about the equipment. Used for operational knowledge capture and equipment-specific context.

description: Current estimated replacement cost for the equipment in base currency. Used for insurance valuation, capital planning, and asset management decisions. Updated periodically to reflect market conditions.

reference: International Association of Ports and Harbors (IAPH) - Terminal Productivity Standards

business_glossary_term: Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) Capture Reference
description: Reference identifier to the ANPR (Automatic Number Plate Recognition) system capture of vehicle license plates for automated gate processing.

Reference to the COPARN EDI message (Container Pre-Announcement) sent prior to gate arrival for pre-clearance and planning.
System timestamp when this gate transaction record was first created in the NAVIS N4 system.
Free-text description of any damage observed on the container, chassis, or cargo during gate processing.
Boolean indicator whether visible damage to the container or cargo was detected during gate inspection.
Government-issued driver license number used for driver identity verification at gate entry.
Full legal name of the driver presenting at the gate for identity verification and security compliance.
Contact telephone number for the driver for operational communication and emergency contact purposes.
Boolean indicator whether the gate clerk manually overrode automated gate processing rules or validations.
System timestamp when this gate transaction record was last updated or modified.
Reference identifier to the OCR (Optical Character Recognition) system capture of container number, license plates, and other visual data at gate.
Free-text explanation provided by the gate clerk for any manual override or exception processing.
Total time in seconds taken to process the gate transaction from entry to exit, used for gate performance KPI measurement.
Actual temperature reading in Celsius from the reefer container monitoring system at gate transaction time.
Target temperature setpoint in Celsius for refrigerated (reefer) containers requiring temperature control.
RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) tag number read from the container or vehicle for automated tracking and identification.
Result of the RPM (Radiation Portal Monitor) scan performed at gate entry for security screening and detection of radioactive materials.
Security seal number affixed to the container door for tamper detection and cargo security verification.
Status of the container seal verification check performed at gate, indicating seal integrity.
Vehicle registration number of the trailer or chassis unit, if separate from the tractor unit.
Externally-visible business identifier for the gate transaction, used for operational reference and audit trails.
Current lifecycle status of the gate transaction indicating processing state and completion.
Precise date and time when the container or vehicle movement through the gate occurred, representing the primary business event time.
Classification of the gate movement type indicating the mode of transport and direction of movement through the terminal gate.
Vehicle registration number of the truck or tractor unit involved in the gate transaction, captured via ANPR (Automatic Number Plate Recognition) or manual entry.
VGM (Verified Gross Mass) of the packed container as required by SOLAS regulations for vessel loading safety.
Gross weight of the container and vehicle measured at the terminal weight bridge in kilograms, used for safety compliance and billing verification.
Assigned yard location (block, row, tier) where the container will be or was stacked in the Container Yard (CY).
Unique identifier for the berth allocation record. Primary key for the berth allocation entity.

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_audit. Business justification: PSC (Port State Control) inspections and ISPS compliance audits are conducted against vessels at specific berth allocations. Linking berth allocation to compliance_audit enables port authority reporti
Reference to the physical berth location allocated for this vessel call. Links to port infrastructure master data.

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Berth allocations for new service startups or infrastructure projects are tracked against internal orders for project cost control. Port finance teams allocate berth preparation and special handling c

Foreign key linking to security.marsec_level_change. Business justification: Berth allocations are operationally constrained by active MARSEC level changes — MARSEC 2/3 declarations impose additional vessel screening, access restrictions, and may modify or cancel allocations.

Reference to the vessel visit for which this berth is allocated. Links to the vessel operations domain.

Foreign key linking to tariff.towage_tariff. Business justification: terminal_berth_allocation.towage_required_flag indicates towage is planned at berth allocation time. The towage_tariff governs the applicable charge. Port billing requires this link to calculate towag

Foreign key linking to booking.call_booking. Business justification: Berth allocation is the terminal's operational response to a call_booking. Terminal planners reference the call_booking to confirm expected TEU volume, crane count, and service windows when creating t

Foreign key linking to vessel.vessel. Business justification: Berth allocation planning requires direct access to vessel LOA, draft, and beam to validate berth compatibility constraints. Terminal planners check vessel_loa_m and vessel_draft_m (already denormaliz

Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: Berth compatibility validation, crane allocation planning, mooring gang sizing, and operational resource forecasting require vessel type specifications before vessel arrival. Critical for berth planni

Foreign key linking to marine.weather_tide_window. Business justification: terminal_berth_allocation has tide_window_required_flag and weather_restriction_flag but no FK to weather_tide_window. Port planners must validate berth windows against tidal constraints — this link i

Foreign key linking to tariff.wharfage_schedule. Business justification: Wharfage charges apply to cargo handled at berth. Berth allocation determines which wharfage schedule applies based on cargo type, vessel type, and berth zone. Essential for cargo-based revenue calcul
Number of Ship-to-Shore (STS) cranes or Quay Cranes (QC) allocated for cargo operations during this berth allocation. Impacts vessel turnaround time and terminal productivity.
Length of quay allocated for this vessel in meters. Must accommodate vessel Length Overall (LOA) plus safety margins.
reference: PIANC (World Association for Waterborne Transport Infrastructure) Standards

description: Free-text notes and comments related to this berth allocation. May include operational instructions, coordination details, or special circumstances.
Business identifier for the berth allocation. Format: BA-YYYYMMDD-NNNN where YYYYMMDD is allocation date and NNNN is sequence number.
Business reason or justification for this specific berth allocation. May include service requirements, vessel preferences, operational constraints, or commercial agreements.

Current lifecycle status of the berth allocation. Planned: initial allocation created; Confirmed: vessel and berth confirmed; Active: vessel currently berthed; Completed: vessel departed; Cancelled: allocation cancelled; Suspended: temporarily on hold.
Actual timestamp when the vessel berthed at the allocated berth. Captured from Vessel Traffic Management System (VTMS) or Terminal Operating System (TOS).
reference: International Maritime Organization (IMO) Port Call Standards

description: Actual timestamp when the vessel departed from the allocated berth. Captured from Vessel Traffic Management System (VTMS) or Terminal Operating System (TOS).
reference: International Maritime Organization (IMO) Port Call Standards

description: Maximum allowable draft at the allocated berth in meters. Vessel draft must not exceed this value for safe berthing operations.
Target container handling productivity in moves per hour for this berth allocation. Used for performance monitoring and SLA compliance.
Planned duration of the berth window in hours. Calculated from berth window start and end times. Used for berth utilization KPIs.
End of the allocated berth window. Defines the latest time the berth is reserved for this vessel before it must be released.
Start of the allocated berth window. Defines the earliest time the berth is reserved for this vessel.
Reason for cancellation if allocation status is cancelled. Includes vessel no-show, schedule changes, operational constraints, or customer request.
Timestamp when this berth allocation was cancelled. Null if allocation has not been cancelled.
Type of cargo operations planned for this berth allocation. LoLo: Lift-on Lift-off (container); RoRo: Roll-on Roll-off (vehicles); Mixed: combination; Bulk: bulk cargo; General: general cargo.
Timestamp when this berth allocation was confirmed by the berth planner and vessel operator. Represents transition from planned to confirmed status.
Type of cranes allocated for this berth. STS: Ship-to-Shore crane; QC: Quay Crane; MHC: Mobile Harbour Crane; Mixed: combination of crane types.
Timestamp when this berth allocation record was first created in the Terminal Operating System. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time Format Standard

description: Planned timestamp when the vessel is estimated to berth at the allocated berth. Used for berth planning and resource scheduling.
reference: International Maritime Organization (IMO) Port Call Standards

description: Planned timestamp when the vessel is estimated to depart from the allocated berth. Used for berth window planning and next vessel scheduling.
reference: International Maritime Organization (IMO) Port Call Standards

description: Estimated total number of container moves (load + discharge) planned for this vessel call. Used for resource planning and productivity forecasting.
Expected total TEU volume to be handled during this berth allocation. Includes both loading and discharge operations.
reference: International Association of Ports and Harbors (IAPH) Standards

business_glossary_term: International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) Cargo Flag
description: Indicates whether this berth allocation involves dangerous goods cargo requiring IMDG compliance. True if dangerous goods are present; False otherwise.
Timestamp when this berth allocation record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time Format Standard

description: Number of mooring gangs allocated for securing the vessel to the berth. Typically ranges from 1 to 4 depending on vessel size and weather conditions.
reference: International Labour Organization (ILO) Maritime Labour Convention

description: Indicates whether marine pilotage services are required for this berth allocation. True if pilotage is mandatory; False if vessel can berth without pilot.
Priority level assigned to this berth allocation. Critical: emergency or VIP vessel; High: priority service; Normal: standard service; Low: flexible scheduling.
Contractual Service Level Agreement turnaround time commitment for this vessel in hours. Measured from ATB to ATD.
System of record that originated this berth allocation. NAVIS_N4: NAVIS N4 TOS; TOPS_EXPERT: TOPS Expert TOS; VTMS: Vessel Traffic Management System; Manual: manually entered.
Any special handling requirements for this berth allocation such as IMDG (International Maritime Dangerous Goods) cargo, refrigerated containers, oversized cargo, or security protocols.
Indicates whether this berth allocation is constrained by tidal conditions. True if vessel can only berth during specific tide windows; False if tide-independent.
Indicates whether towage (tug) services are required for this berth allocation. True if tugs are needed for berthing/unberthing; False otherwise.
Maximum draft of the vessel in meters. Must be validated against berth depth restrictions to ensure safe berthing.
Length Overall of the vessel in meters. Used to validate berth allocation suitability and quay length requirements.
Indicates whether this berth allocation has weather-related restrictions (wind speed, wave height, visibility). True if weather-sensitive; False otherwise.
Unique identifier for the vessel bay plan record. Primary key for vessel stowage and operational planning.
Reference to the berth allocation record specifying where the vessel will dock.

Reference to the specific vessel call or visit for which this bay plan applies.

Deadline timestamp by which all export cargo must be delivered to the terminal for loading onto this vessel call.
Planned sequence order for discharging this container from the vessel to optimize operational efficiency.
Vessel hatch identifier for grouping bays and planning hatch cover operations and crane work sequences.
Planned operational sequence for working vessel hatches to optimize crane productivity and minimize vessel turnaround time.
Planned sequence order for loading this container onto the vessel to optimize operational efficiency and vessel stability.
Indicates whether the container or cargo exceeds standard ISO dimensions requiring special handling and stowage considerations.
Front overhang dimension in centimeters for out-of-gauge cargo. Null for standard containers.
Left side overhang dimension in centimeters for out-of-gauge cargo. Null for standard containers.
Rear overhang dimension in centimeters for out-of-gauge cargo. Null for standard containers.
Right side overhang dimension in centimeters for out-of-gauge cargo. Null for standard containers.
Height overhang dimension in centimeters for out-of-gauge cargo. Null for standard containers.
Timestamp when this vessel bay plan was formally approved and released for operational execution.
Timestamp when this vessel bay plan record was initially created in the terminal operating system.
Current lifecycle status of the vessel bay plan indicating planning and execution stage.
Timestamp when this vessel bay plan record was last modified or revised.
Version number of the bay plan to track revisions and updates to the stowage plan throughout the vessel call lifecycle.
Number of STS (Ship-to-Shore) or QC (Quay Crane) units allocated for this vessel operation to achieve target productivity.
Number of stevedore gangs or labor teams allocated for vessel loading and discharge operations.
Scheduled timestamp for completing all vessel cargo operations including final crane move and securing.
Scheduled timestamp for commencing vessel cargo operations including first crane move.
UN/LOCODE for the port where this container is scheduled to be discharged from the vessel.
UN/LOCODE for the port where this container was loaded onto the vessel.
Indicates whether containers require pre-marshalling or repositioning in the yard prior to vessel loading to optimize load sequence.
Indicates whether the container is a refrigerated unit requiring temperature control and power connection.
Required temperature setting in Celsius for refrigerated containers. Null for non-reefer units.
Free-text notes documenting equipment allocation decisions, operational constraints, or special resource requirements for this vessel call.
Indicates whether this container requires restowing or repositioning on the vessel to access underlying cargo or optimize stowage.
Transverse position identifier on the vessel indicating the row location for container stowage.
Container seal number for security and customs verification purposes.
reference: ISO 17712 Freight Container Seals, WCO SAFE Framework

description: Special handling or stowage instructions for the container including segregation requirements, securing notes, or operational constraints.
reference: SMDG BAPLIE 3.x Standard, IMDG Code Segregation Requirements

description: Total TEU count for this vessel call bay plan representing standardized container volume measurement.
Vertical position identifier on the vessel indicating the tier or stack level for container stowage.
Total number of container discharge moves planned for this vessel call across all bays and hatches.
Total number of container load moves planned for this vessel call across all bays and hatches.
Indicates whether this container is transhipment cargo that will be transferred to another vessel at this port.
United Nations number identifying hazardous substances and articles for dangerous goods. Null for non-hazardous cargo.
reference: UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods

Reference to the vessel visit during which this container was discharged or is scheduled to be loaded. Links container to vessel operations for vessel-mode arrivals/departures.

Foreign key linking to booking.slot_reservation. Business justification: A slot_reservation authorizes a specific vessel stowage position for a container. When the container is loaded, the container_visit must confirm against the slot_reservation for load list validation,

Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal. Business justification: A container visit lifecycle (gate-in through gate-out or vessel discharge/load) occurs at a specific terminal. While terminal can be derived via yard_slot -> yard_block -> terminal, a direct terminal_

Foreign key linking to intermodal.transport_order. Business justification: Container visits execute transport orders in multimodal supply chains. Linking enables end-to-end shipment visibility, transport order status updates, and exception management when containers miss sch

Foreign key linking to infrastructure.weighing_station. Business justification: SOLAS VGM regulatory compliance requires tracing which certified weighing_station captured the container's verified gross mass. container_visit stores vgm_weight_kg and vgm_method but has no FK to the

Foreign key linking to tariff.wharfage_schedule. Business justification: Wharfage is charged on cargo passing over the wharf per container visit. The wharfage_schedule governs the applicable rate by cargo type, container type, and trade direction. Port revenue reporting an

Foreign key linking to terminal.yard_slot. Business justification: container_visit has current_yard_location (STRING) and yard_block (STRING) which are denormalized representations of the container's current position. Should FK to yard_slot for authoritative location
Transportation mode by which the container arrived at the terminal. Determines gate processing, yard allocation, and intermodal handling requirements.
Carrier booking reference number for the container shipment. Links container to commercial booking and shipping instructions.
High-level classification of cargo carried in the container. Determines handling requirements, equipment assignment, and yard allocation strategy.
Name of the consignee (importer) for this container shipment. Sourced from BOL or booking documentation.
reference: DCSA - Bill of Lading Data Model

description: Unique container identification number following ISO 6346 standard format (4 letters + 7 digits). The globally unique identifier for the physical container unit.
reference: ISO 6346 - Freight containers - Coding, identification and marking

description: Date and time when this container visit record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.
Free-text description of container damage if damage_flag is true. Documents condition for repair estimates and liability determination.
Indicates whether the container has reported damage. Triggers inspection workflow and potential repair work orders.
Delivery order number authorizing release of the container to the consignee or their agent. Required document for import container gate-out.
Date when demurrage charges begin to accrue for this container visit. Calculated as arrival date plus free time days.
Planned or actual transportation mode for container departure from the terminal. Null until departure is scheduled or executed.

Total time in hours that the container has remained in the terminal from arrival to current moment (or to departure if departed). Key performance indicator for terminal efficiency and customer service.
UN/LOCODE of the final destination for the container cargo. May differ from POD for transshipment or intermodal movements.
reference: UN/LOCODE - United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations

description: Number of days of free storage time allowed before demurrage or detention charges begin. Defined by tariff or commercial agreement.
reference: Terminal Tariff - Storage and Demurrage Rules

description: Indicates whether the container is loaded with cargo (full) or empty. Critical for weight calculations, handling procedures, and billing.
Date and time when the container physically entered the terminal through the gate. Marks the start of terminal custody and dwell time calculation for truck/rail arrivals.
Date and time when the container physically exited the terminal through the gate. Marks the end of terminal custody and completes dwell time calculation for truck/rail departures.
Description of OOG measurements if oog_flag is true. Specifies overhang dimensions (front, back, left, right, top) in standard format for equipment planning.
Indicates whether the container or its cargo exceeds standard ISO dimensions (out-of-gauge). Requires special handling equipment and yard allocation.
UN/LOCODE of the port where the container is to be discharged from the vessel. Five-character code per UN/LOCODE standard.
reference: UN/LOCODE - United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations

description: UN/LOCODE of the port where the container was loaded onto the vessel. Five-character code per UN/LOCODE standard.
reference: UN/LOCODE - United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations

description: Indicates whether the container is a refrigerated (reefer) unit requiring temperature control and power supply during terminal stay.
Target relative humidity setting for refrigerated container as percentage. Required for certain cargo types (e.g., fresh produce) to prevent spoilage.
Target temperature setting for refrigerated container in degrees Celsius. Critical for cargo preservation; monitored continuously by terminal reefer management system.
Ventilation configuration for reefer container (e.g., closed, 10 CBM/hr, 20 CBM/hr). Controls fresh air exchange rate for perishable cargo.
RFID tag identifier attached to the container for automated tracking and gate processing. Enables touchless identification at gate and yard checkpoints.
Primary security seal number affixed to the container door. Used for customs verification and cargo security tracking per ISPS Code requirements.
Secondary or customs seal number if multiple seals are applied. Common for high-security shipments or specific customs requirements.
Name of the shipper (exporter) for this container shipment. Sourced from BOL or booking documentation.
reference: DCSA - Bill of Lading Data Model

description: Empty weight of the container itself in kilograms, excluding cargo. Used for VGM Method 2 calculations and payload verification.
TEU conversion factor for the container. Standard values: 1.0 for 20ft containers, 2.0 for 40ft containers, 2.25 for 45ft containers. Used for capacity planning and billing calculations.
Native identifier for this container visit in the source Terminal Operating System (NAVIS N4 or TOPS Expert). Used for system integration and audit trail.
reference: NAVIS N4 / TOPS Expert - System Integration

description: Four-digit UN number identifying the specific dangerous goods substance (e.g., UN1203 for gasoline). Required for hazmat containers per IMDG Code.
reference: IMDG Code - United Nations Dangerous Goods Classification

description: Date and time when this container visit record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.
Date and time when the container was discharged from the vessel onto the terminal. Applicable for vessel arrival mode; marks start of terminal custody for vessel-delivered containers.
Date and time when the container was loaded onto the vessel from the terminal. Applicable for vessel departure mode; marks end of terminal custody for vessel-bound containers.
Method used to determine VGM: Method 1 (weighing packed container) or Method 2 (weighing contents and adding tare weight). Required for SOLAS compliance documentation.

business_glossary_term: Verified Gross Mass (VGM) Weight in Kilograms
description: Verified Gross Mass of the packed container in kilograms, as required by SOLAS regulation VI/2. Mandatory for all full export containers before vessel loading.
Current lifecycle status of the container visit within the terminal. Tracks progression from arrival through departure, including hold states for customs or operational reasons.
Primary key for reefer_monitoring

Foreign key linking to billing.charge_event. Business justification: Reefer monitoring sessions generate reefer electricity and monitoring surcharge charge events. Billing must trace each reefer charge_event to the originating monitoring record for reefer power billing

Foreign key linking to masterdata.commodity_code. Business justification: Cold chain compliance validation, temperature range verification against commodity specifications, and cargo-specific monitoring protocols require HS code reference. Essential for perishable cargo qua
Foreign key reference to the reefer container being monitored. Links to the container master data.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Reefer monitoring operations (power consumption, technician callouts, alarm responses) generate costs allocated to a reefer operations cost centre. Port finance teams track reefer power and service co
