The measured actual temperature inside the reefer container at the time of monitoring in degrees Celsius. Critical for cold chain integrity verification.
Boolean indicator whether an alarm condition was triggered during this monitoring event. True indicates an alarm was raised, False indicates normal operation.
The severity level of the alarm condition. Critical requires immediate action, high requires urgent attention, medium requires scheduled intervention, low is informational.

The category of alarm condition detected. Temperature deviation indicates actual temperature outside acceptable range, power failure indicates loss of electrical supply, door open indicates container door unsealed, humidity deviation indicates moisture level out of range, CO2 deviation indicates gas concentration issue, equipment malfunction indicates refrigeration unit failure.
The classification of perishable cargo being transported in the reefer container. Examples include frozen meat, fresh produce, pharmaceuticals, dairy products, flowers.
The carbon dioxide concentration measured inside the reefer container in percentage. Critical for controlled atmosphere cargo such as fruits and vegetables.

Boolean indicator whether this monitoring event confirms cold chain integrity compliance. True indicates temperature and conditions within acceptable range, False indicates breach of cold chain requirements.
The cumulative operating hours of the refrigeration compressor since last reset. Used for maintenance scheduling and equipment lifecycle management.
The operational status of the refrigeration compressor unit. Running indicates active cooling, stopped indicates unit off, standby indicates ready but not cooling, fault indicates malfunction.
Free text description of the corrective action taken or required in response to this monitoring event. Populated when intervention was necessary.
Boolean indicator whether this monitoring event requires corrective action or intervention. True indicates follow-up action needed.
The date and time when this monitoring record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.
The status of the automatic defrost cycle for the refrigeration unit. Active indicates defrost in progress, inactive indicates normal cooling mode, scheduled indicates defrost pending.
The physical status of the reefer container door. Closed indicates door shut but not sealed, open indicates door ajar or open, sealed indicates door closed with customs or security seal intact.
The relative humidity percentage measured inside the reefer container. Important for cargo types sensitive to moisture levels such as fresh produce.
The date and time when this monitoring record was last modified in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.

The method or system that captured this monitoring reading. Automated sensor indicates TOS integration, manual inspection indicates technician reading, remote telemetry indicates IoT device, RFID reader indicates tag-based monitoring.

The current lifecycle status of this monitoring event. Active indicates ongoing monitoring, resolved indicates issue closed, escalated indicates elevated to management, pending review indicates awaiting technician assessment.
The date and time when the reefer container monitoring reading was captured. Principal business event timestamp for this monitoring transaction.
Boolean indicator whether an alert notification was sent to stakeholders for this monitoring event. True indicates notification dispatched to customer or operations team.
The date and time when alert notification was sent to stakeholders regarding this monitoring event. Null if no notification was sent.
The oxygen concentration measured inside the reefer container in percentage. Used for controlled atmosphere monitoring to preserve perishable cargo.
The electrical power connection status of the reefer container. Connected indicates active power supply, disconnected indicates no power, standby indicates backup power mode.
The electrical voltage being supplied to the reefer container in volts. Standard voltages are 220V, 380V, or 440V depending on terminal infrastructure.
Free text field for additional notes, observations, or comments related to this reefer monitoring event. Used by technicians and operations staff for context.
The unique identifier of the monitoring sensor or telemetry device that captured this reading. Used for device calibration tracking and data quality assurance.
The target temperature setting configured for the reefer container in degrees Celsius. This is the temperature the refrigeration unit is programmed to maintain.
Boolean indicator whether this monitoring event represents a breach of customer service level agreement for reefer monitoring. True indicates SLA breach occurred.
Reference number or identifier linking this monitoring event to a technician inspection work order or maintenance record. Populated when manual intervention occurred.
The calculated difference between set temperature and actual temperature in degrees. Positive values indicate warmer than set point, negative values indicate cooler than set point.
The unit of measure for temperature readings. Standard is Celsius but Fahrenheit may be used in some jurisdictions.
The air exchange rate in cubic meters per hour when ventilation is active. Applicable for cargo requiring fresh air circulation.
The ventilation mode configured for the reefer container. Closed for frozen cargo, open for fresh air exchange, controlled for modified atmosphere.

Reference to the container currently occupying this slot. Null if slot is vacant. Enables real-time container location tracking.

Reference to the parent yard block containing this slot. Links slot to its physical block location within the terminal.
Operational accessibility rating based on equipment reach, traffic flow, and physical constraints. Affects slot utilization priority.
Indicates whether the slot is currently active and available for operational use. False if decommissioned or permanently blocked.
Bay coordinate within the yard block. Represents the lateral position of the slot in the stacking grid.
Date when the slot was first commissioned and made available for container storage operations.
Date and time when this slot record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.
Indicates whether the slot is located within a designated customs inspection area. True if subject to enhanced customs controls.
Date when the slot was decommissioned and removed from active service. Null if still active.
Distance from this slot to the nearest terminal gate in meters. Used for optimizing container placement and reducing truck turn time.
Distance from this slot to the nearest quay crane position in meters. Critical for optimizing vessel loading/discharge operations.
Condition of drainage infrastructure at the slot. Critical for reefer operations and preventing water damage to cargo.

Maximum vertical clearance available at this slot position. Critical for high-cube containers and OOG cargo.
Date of the most recent safety and structural inspection of the slot. Used for maintenance scheduling and compliance tracking.
Date and time when a container was last placed in this slot. Used for slot utilization analytics and dwell time calculations.

Physical length of the slot in meters. Determines the maximum container size that can be placed (e.g., 20ft, 40ft, 45ft).

Maximum gross weight the slot can safely support in kilograms. Derived from ground bearing capacity and structural limits.
Scheduled date for the next mandatory inspection of the slot. Ensures compliance with safety and operational standards.
Indicates whether the slot is currently occupied by a container. True if a container is present, false if vacant.

Logical operational zone or area code within the terminal. Used for yard segmentation and equipment dispatch optimization.
Indicates whether the slot is equipped with a reefer power plug for refrigerated containers. True if plug is installed and operational.
Type of electrical connection available for reefer containers. Specifies voltage and phase configuration of the power supply.
Voltage rating of the reefer power supply at this slot in volts. Common values are 220V, 380V, or 440V.
Date and time when the current slot reservation expires. After expiry, the slot becomes available for re-allocation.
Indicates whether the slot has been reserved for an incoming container. Supports yard planning and berth discharge operations.
Unique identifier of the RFID tag installed at this slot for automated container tracking. Null if no RFID infrastructure.
Row coordinate within the yard block. Represents the longitudinal position of the slot in the stacking grid.
Unique alphanumeric code identifying the slot within the terminal. Used for operational reference and TOS integration.
Current operational status of the slot. Indicates whether the slot is available for container placement or restricted.

Container capacity of the slot expressed in TEU. Standard slots are 1.0 TEU (20ft) or 2.0 TEU (40ft).
Tier (height level) coordinate within the yard block. Represents the vertical stacking position, with 1 being ground level.
Date and time when this slot record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking and data quality monitoring.
Physical width of the slot in meters. Standard container width is 2.44m (8ft), but slots may accommodate wider OOG cargo.

Overall outcome of the PSC inspection. No deficiencies indicates a clean inspection. Deficiencies rectified indicates issues were resolved before departure. description: Indicates whether the BAPLIE EDI message containing the vessel stowage plan has been received from the shipping line.

description: Sequential port-assigned identifier for this specific vessel visit. Used as the business reference number for all port operations and billing.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning Module

description: Current lifecycle status of the port call. Tracks progression from pre-arrival notification through departure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_arrival|expected|arrived|berthed|working|completed|departed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning Module

description: Timestamp when all cargo loading or discharge operations were completed. Used for calculating working time and productivity.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning Module

description: Timestamp when cargo loading or discharge operations commenced. Used for calculating working time and productivity.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning Module

description: Timestamp when this port call record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning Module

description: Current status of customs clearance for this port call. Affects cargo release and vessel departure authorization.
reference: Port Community System (PCS); World Customs Organization (WCO)

description: Indicates whether the vessel is carrying IMDG-classified dangerous goods during this call. Triggers additional safety and documentation requirements.
Estimated timestamp when the vessel is expected to arrive at the port anchorage or pilot boarding station. Updated as voyage progresses.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning Module; Vessel Traffic Management System (VTMS)

description: Estimated timestamp when the vessel is expected to be secured alongside the berth and ready for cargo operations.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning Module

description: Estimated timestamp when the vessel is expected to depart from the berth and leave the port.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning Module

description: Timestamp when the first mooring line was secured to the berth during berthing operations.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning Module

description: Timestamp when the gangway was lowered and secured, allowing personnel access to the vessel.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning Module

business_glossary_term: International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Security Level
description: ISPS security level in effect during this port call (1=normal, 2=heightened, 3=exceptional). Determines security procedures and access controls.
reference: International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Code

description: Timestamp when the last mooring line was released during unberthing operations. Marks start of departure sequence.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning Module

description: Timestamp when this port call record was last updated. Audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning Module

description: Timestamp when the marine pilot boarded the vessel to guide it into port. First milestone in the arrival sequence.
reference: Vessel Traffic Management System (VTMS); NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System

description: Timestamp when the marine pilot disembarked after guiding the vessel out of port. Final milestone in the departure sequence.
reference: Vessel Traffic Management System (VTMS); NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System

description: UN/LOCODE of the next port where cargo will be discharged after this call. Five-character code following UN/LOCODE standard.
reference: UN/LOCODE - United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations

description: UN/LOCODE of the port where cargo was loaded prior to this call. Five-character code following UN/LOCODE standard.
reference: UN/LOCODE - United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations

description: Indicates whether this vessel was selected for Port State Control inspection during this call. True if inspection occurred.

Primary reason for the vessel visit. Determines operational requirements and resource allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: loading|discharge|loading_discharge|bunkering|transit|lay_up|crew_change|repair|provisions — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning Module

description: Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or exceptions related to this port call.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning Module

description: Total number of FEU declared for loading and discharge operations during this call. Used for capacity planning and billing.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning Module

description: Total number of TEU declared for loading and discharge operations during this call. Used for capacity planning and billing.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning Module

description: Reference number issued by VTS granting clearance for vessel movement within port waters. Required for compliance with ISPS and port regulations.
reference: Vessel Traffic Management System (VTMS); International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Code

Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Call schedules drive proforma disbursement account (DA) preparation — call_schedule has proforma_disbursement_account_required flag. The applicable port_tariff is required to estimate port charges in

Foreign key linking to masterdata.shipping_line. Business justification: Call schedules are submitted by shipping lines; the port must resolve the EDI partner, commercial account, and tariff group from the schedule. Normalizing operating_shipping_line to masterdata.shippin
Reference to the vessel scheduled for this port call. Links to the vessel master data.

Foreign key linking to vessel.voyage. Business justification: call_schedule represents planned port calls for a vessel over a service route, and each planned call is associated with a specific voyage. call_schedule.voyage_number is a denormalized STRING copy of
Name of the shipping alliance if this service is part of a carrier alliance (e.g., 2M, Ocean Alliance, THE Alliance). Null for independent services.

business_glossary_term: Average Vessel Capacity Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit (TEU)
description: Average container capacity in TEU of vessels deployed on this service. Used for capacity planning and commercial forecasting.
reference: ISO 668 - Container Classification and TEU Standards

description: Preferred berth or berth zone requested by the shipping line or agent for this scheduled call. Used in berth allocation planning.
Reference to the commercial service agreement or contract governing this scheduled service. Links to tariff and SLA terms.

business_glossary_term: Forecast Cargo Volume Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit (TEU)
description: Forecasted container volume in TEU expected to be loaded and discharged at this port call. Used for yard planning and equipment allocation.
reference: ISO 668 - Container Classification and TEU Standards

description: Forecasted number of TEU to be discharged from the vessel at this port call.
reference: ISO 668 - Container Classification and TEU Standards

description: Forecasted number of TEU to be loaded onto the vessel at this port call.
reference: ISO 668 - Container Classification and TEU Standards

description: Forecasted number of TEU requiring restow operations (containers moved to optimize stowage). Impacts operational planning and berth time.
UN/LOCODE of the port immediately following this call in the rotation sequence. Used for cargo routing and stowage planning.
reference: UN/LOCODE - United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations

description: Explanation for why a scheduled port call was omitted or skipped (e.g., weather, operational delays, insufficient cargo). Populated when schedule_status is omitted.
Expected duration in hours that the vessel will occupy the berth, calculated from planned ETB to planned ETD. Used for berth utilization planning.
Scheduled estimated time when the vessel is expected to arrive at the port. Forward-looking planning timestamp provided by shipping line or agent.
reference: IMO FAL Convention - Arrival Notification Standards

description: Scheduled estimated time when the vessel is expected to berth at the assigned terminal. Used for berth planning and resource allocation.
Scheduled estimated time when the vessel is expected to depart from the port. Used for berth window planning and next port coordination.
reference: IMO FAL Convention - Departure Notification Standards

description: Five-letter UN/LOCODE identifying the port for this scheduled call (e.g., USNYC, SGSIN, NLRTM).
reference: UN/LOCODE - United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations

description: UN/LOCODE of the port immediately preceding this call in the rotation sequence. Used for voyage tracking and cargo routing.
reference: UN/LOCODE - United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations

description: Priority classification for this scheduled call. Higher priority calls receive preferential berth allocation and resource assignment.
User ID or system identifier that created this schedule record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Timestamp when this schedule record was first created in the port system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
User ID or system identifier that last modified this schedule record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Timestamp when this schedule record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail.
Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or operational comments related to this scheduled call.
Total number of ports in the complete service rotation. Provides context for the rotation sequence number.
Sequential position of this port call within the service rotation. Indicates the order of port visits in the voyage loop (e.g., 1 for first port, 2 for second).
Date from which this schedule version becomes effective. Supports schedule versioning and historical tracking.
reference: Port Community System (PCS) Schedule Versioning Standards

description: Date until which this schedule version remains effective. Null for current active schedules. Used for schedule supersession management.
reference: Port Community System (PCS) Schedule Versioning Standards

description: Date when this schedule was first published or communicated to the port. Used for schedule horizon management and planning lead time analysis.
reference: Port Community System (PCS) Schedule Publication Standards

description: Timestamp when this schedule record was received by the port system. Used for audit trail and schedule update tracking.
reference: Port Community System (PCS) Data Receipt Standards

description: Origin system or channel through which this schedule information was received (e.g., EDI from shipping line, agent portal submission, VTS pre-notification).
reference: EDI IFTMIN - Instruction Message for Transport

description: Current status of this scheduled port call. Tracks the lifecycle from initial provisional schedule through confirmation, amendments, or cancellation.
reference: Port Community System (PCS) Schedule Management Standards

description: Version identifier for this schedule record (e.g., 2024-01-V01). Allows tracking of schedule amendments and revisions over time.
reference: Port Community System (PCS) Schedule Versioning Standards

description: Number of vessels deployed in this service rotation. Indicates the fleet size required to maintain the service frequency.
Primary key for draft_survey

Foreign key linking to tariff.wharfage_schedule. Business justification: Draft surveys determine cargo weight (calculated_cargo_weight_mt) which directly drives wharfage charges under weight-based tariff schedules. Essential for wharfage billing calculation, cargo tonnage-

Foreign key linking to infrastructure.berth. Business justification: Draft surveys require berth-specific water depth and tidal data for accurate displacement calculations. Surveyors record the exact berth where the survey was conducted. Cargo weight certification and

Foreign key linking to masterdata.cargo_category. Business justification: Draft surveys verify cargo weight for specific cargo categories (bulk, liquid bulk, general cargo). Linking to the cargo_category master supports weight verification reporting by cargo type, wharfage

Foreign key linking to billing.charge_event. Business justification: Draft survey results directly trigger wharfage charge events based on calculated_cargo_weight_mt. This is a named billing trigger in bulk cargo port operations. Enables traceability from charge event

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Draft surveys performed by port surveyors generate costs allocated to marine services cost centres. Required for survey service cost allocation, operational expense tracking, and profitability analysi

Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Draft surveys determine cargo weight for wharfage invoice calculation. Direct FK enables auditors to verify invoice amounts against survey-calculated cargo weight, a mandatory reconciliation step in p
Reference to the port call during which this draft survey was conducted. Links the survey to the vessel's visit.
reference: NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System - Vessel Planning

description: Foreign key linking to cargo.shipment. Business justification: Draft surveys verify cargo weight against declared shipment weight for freight billing disputes, cargo insurance claims, and port dues calculation. Draft survey→shipment FK enables direct reconciliati

Foreign key linking to marine.survey_appointment. Business justification: A draft survey is scheduled and authorized via a survey_appointment. Linking draft_survey to survey_appointment enables end-to-end traceability from appointment scheduling through survey execution and

Foreign key linking to marine.surveyor. Business justification: Draft surveys must reference the licensed surveyor who performed them for regulatory traceability — cargo weight certification, P&I club disputes, and customs verification all require surveyor identit
Reference to the vessel on which the draft survey was performed.
reference: International Maritime Organization (IMO) Ship Identification Number Scheme

description: Draft reading at the aft perpendicular on the port side of the vessel, measured in meters from the waterline to the keel.
Draft reading at the aft perpendicular on the starboard side of the vessel, measured in meters from the waterline to the keel.
Weight of ballast water on board the vessel at the time of survey, measured in metric tons. Deducted from displacement to calculate cargo weight.
reference: International Convention for the Control and Management of Ships' Ballast Water and Sediments

description: Weight of bunker fuel oil on board the vessel at the time of survey, measured in metric tons. Deducted from displacement to calculate cargo weight.

Net weight of cargo loaded or discharged, calculated by subtracting lightship weight, constant, bunkers, water, ballast, and stores from the displacement. Measured in metric tons. This is the principal quantitative result of the draft survey.
reference: International Association of Marine Surveyors - Draft Survey Standards

description: Date on which the draft survey certificate was officially issued by the surveyor.
Indicates whether a formal draft survey certificate has been issued for this survey. True if certificate issued, False otherwise.

Constant correction factor representing the difference between the actual lightship weight and the documented lightship weight, measured in metric tons. Accounts for permanent additions or removals since last lightship survey.
reference: International Association of Marine Surveyors - Draft Survey Standards

description: Timestamp when this draft survey record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.
Total weight of water displaced by the vessel at the surveyed draft, measured in metric tons. Derived from hydrostatic tables and draft readings.
reference: International Association of Marine Surveyors - Draft Survey Standards

description: Dock water allowance correction applied when the vessel is in water of intermediate density between salt and fresh water, measured in millimeters.
Draft reading at the forward perpendicular on the port side of the vessel, measured in meters from the waterline to the keel.
Draft reading at the forward perpendicular on the starboard side of the vessel, measured in meters from the waterline to the keel.
Weight of fresh water on board the vessel at the time of survey, measured in metric tons. Deducted from displacement to calculate cargo weight.
reference: International Association of Marine Surveyors - Draft Survey Standards

description: Fresh water allowance correction applied when the vessel is in fresh water or dock water, measured in millimeters. Accounts for the difference in buoyancy between salt and fresh water.
Timestamp when this draft survey record was last updated in the system. Audit trail for record modifications.
Weight of the vessel in light condition (empty, without cargo, fuel, water, stores), measured in metric tons. Used as baseline for cargo weight calculation.
Transverse inclination of the vessel measured in degrees. Positive value indicates list to starboard, negative indicates list to port.
reference: International Association of Marine Surveyors - Draft Survey Standards

description: Average of port and starboard aft draft readings, used in displacement calculations.
reference: International Association of Marine Surveyors - Draft Survey Standards

description: Overall mean draft of the vessel calculated from forward, midship, and aft draft readings, used as the primary input for displacement calculation.
reference: International Association of Marine Surveyors - Draft Survey Standards

description: Average of port and starboard forward draft readings, used in displacement calculations.
reference: International Association of Marine Surveyors - Draft Survey Standards

description: Average of port and starboard midship draft readings, used in displacement calculations.
reference: International Association of Marine Surveyors - Draft Survey Standards

description: Draft reading at the midship section on the port side of the vessel, measured in meters from the waterline to the keel.
Draft reading at the midship section on the starboard side of the vessel, measured in meters from the waterline to the keel.
Additional notes, observations, or comments recorded by the surveyor regarding the draft survey, including any anomalies, special conditions, or clarifications.
Standardized code representing sea state conditions during the survey (e.g., Douglas Sea Scale 0-9). Affects survey accuracy and reliability.
reference: World Meteorological Organization (WMO) - Sea State Code

description: Weight of stores, provisions, and consumables on board the vessel at the time of survey, measured in metric tons. Deducted from displacement to calculate cargo weight.
reference: International Association of Marine Surveyors - Draft Survey Standards

description: Surveyor's assessment of the accuracy and reliability of the draft survey results based on conditions and measurement quality: excellent, good, fair, or poor.
reference: International Association of Marine Surveyors - Quality Standards

description: Date and time when the draft survey was conducted. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.

Business purpose for conducting the draft survey: cargo weight verification, load line compliance check, demurrage calculation support, charter party contractual requirement, customs declaration, or insurance claim documentation.
reference: International Association of Ports and Harbors (IAPH) - Cargo Handling Standards

description: External reference number or certificate number assigned by the surveyor or survey company for this draft survey.
Current lifecycle status of the draft survey: draft (in progress), completed (survey finished), certified (certificate issued), disputed (under dispute), or cancelled.
Type of draft survey conducted: initial (before loading), final (after loading/discharge), intermediate (during operations), pre-loading, or post-discharge.
reference: International Association of Marine Surveyors - Draft Survey Standards

description: Difference between forward and aft drafts. Positive value indicates trim by stern (aft deeper), negative indicates trim by bow (forward deeper).
reference: International Association of Marine Surveyors - Draft Survey Standards

business_glossary_term: Water Density (Kilograms per Cubic Meter - kg/m³)
description: Density of the water in which the vessel is floating at the time of survey, measured in kilograms per cubic meter. Used for displacement corrections.
reference: International Association of Marine Surveyors - Draft Survey Standards

description: Description of weather conditions at the time of the survey (e.g., calm, moderate wind, rough seas). Weather can affect draft reading accuracy.
Unique system identifier for the port call record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to the port location where the call occurred

Foreign key linking to the vessel making the port call
Indicates whether this vessel has access restrictions or blacklist status for this specific port location.
Timestamp when the vessel arrived at the port location (pilot boarding or berth arrival depending on location type).

Total duration of the port call in hours, calculated from arrival to departure. Used for performance analytics and berth utilization metrics.
Indicates whether this port location is a preferred calling point for this vessel based on operational history and performance.
Number of deficiencies identified during PSC inspection if conducted. Used for vessel risk profiling.
Indicates whether a Port State Control inspection was conducted during this call.
Explanation of why access restrictions apply to this vessel-location combination (e.g., PSC detention history, commercial dispute, safety incident).
Total port charges billed for this call including berth dues, pilotage, towage, and other marine services.
Unique identifier for the voyage record. Primary key for the voyage entity.

Foreign key linking to customer.port_community_participant. Business justification: Voyage billing, SLA tracking, and commercial reporting require linking each voyage to the operating shipping line's port community participant record. voyage.shipping_line_id references masterdata but

Reference to the shipping line or carrier operating this voyage. Links to the participant account representing the commercial operator responsible for the voyage.

Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal. Business justification: Voyage rotation planning and terminal capacity forecasting require linking each voyage to its calling terminal. Terminal operations teams use voyage-level data for advance resource allocation and thro

Foreign key linking to tariff.towage_tariff. Business justification: voyage has towage_required_flag indicating towage is anticipated. Pre-arrival proforma DA preparation requires the applicable towage_tariff to estimate costs. Port agents and shipping lines use voyage
Reference to the vessel performing this voyage. Links to the vessel master data containing vessel profile, IMO number, LOA, DWT, GRT, NRT, and other vessel characteristics.
The actual timestamp when the vessel arrived at the port limits or pilot boarding station. Recorded by Vessel Traffic Service (VTS) and used for performance measurement and billing calculations.
reference: IMO FAL Convention - Actual Arrival Reporting

description: The actual timestamp when the vessel was secured alongside the berth with first line ashore. Marks the start of berth occupancy time for billing and the commencement of cargo operations window.
reference: IAPH Port Operations - Berth Occupancy Standards

description: The actual timestamp when the vessel departed from the berth with last line off. Marks the end of berth occupancy time for billing and the conclusion of the port call.
reference: IMO FAL Convention - Actual Departure Reporting

description: Boolean indicator showing whether the BAPLIE (Bayplan and Stowage Plan) EDI message has been received. BAPLIE contains the vessel's container stowage plan showing exact position of each container on board.
The target container handling productivity rate for this voyage measured in container moves per hour. Used for terminal resource planning and SLA performance measurement.
reference: NAVIS N4 Berth Planning - Productivity Standards

description: Sequential number indicating this port's position in the voyage's port call sequence. Used to order intermediate ports of call and support cargo routing logic.
Boolean indicator showing whether the cargo manifest for this voyage has been received from the shipping line. Critical for customs clearance, cargo planning, and terminal readiness.
reference: WCO SAFE Framework - Advance Cargo Information

description: Boolean indicator showing whether the COPARN (Container Pre-Announcement) EDI message has been received. COPARN provides advance notice of containers to be discharged and loaded.
The timestamp when this voyage record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

Current status of customs clearance for the voyage cargo. Pending indicates awaiting customs processing, cleared indicates all cargo approved for release, inspection_required indicates physical examination needed, hold indicates customs detention, released indicates cargo available for delivery.
reference: WCO Revised Kyoto Convention - Customs Procedures

description: UN/LOCODE of the voyage destination port. Five-character code identifying the final port of call for this voyage leg.
The number of empty containers onboard measured in TEU. Critical for empty container repositioning planning and depot management.
reference: ISO 668 - Container Classification and TEU Standard

description: The date when the voyage concluded at the destination port. Represents the actual or planned arrival date marking the end of the commercial voyage journey.

The estimated timestamp when the vessel is expected to arrive at the port. Updated throughout the voyage based on vessel position, speed, and weather conditions. Critical for berth planning and resource allocation.
reference: IMO FAL Convention - Estimated Arrival Reporting

description: The estimated timestamp when the vessel is expected to berth at the assigned quay. Accounts for pilotage, towage, and berth availability. Used for terminal operations planning and gate scheduling.
reference: IAPH Port Operations - Berth Planning Standards

description: The estimated timestamp when the vessel is expected to depart from the berth. Based on cargo operations completion forecast, customs clearance, and next port schedule requirements.
reference: IMO FAL Convention - Estimated Departure Reporting

business_glossary_term: IMDG (International Maritime Dangerous Goods) Cargo Onboard Flag
description: Boolean indicator showing whether the vessel is carrying dangerous goods as classified under IMDG Code. Triggers special safety protocols, segregation requirements, and emergency response planning.
reference: IMO IMDG Code - Dangerous Goods Classification

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this is the vessel's first-ever call to this port. Maiden calls often receive special ceremonial recognition and may have different operational procedures.
reference: IAPH Port Operations - Vessel Call Classification

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this scheduled port call was omitted or skipped by the vessel. Used for schedule reliability analysis and customer service impact assessment.

business_glossary_term: ISPS (International Ship and Port Facility Security) Security Level
description: The ISPS security level applicable to this voyage. Level 1 is normal security, Level 2 is heightened security due to increased risk, Level 3 is exceptional security measures for imminent threat. Determines security protocols and access controls.
reference: IMO ISPS Code - Maritime Security Levels

description: The number of laden (full) containers onboard measured in TEU. Used for cargo volume analysis and vessel utilization calculations.
reference: ISO 668 - Container Classification and TEU Standard

description: The timestamp when this voyage record was last modified. Updated whenever any attribute value changes. Critical for change tracking and data synchronization.
UN/LOCODE of the next scheduled port of call after the current port. Critical for stowage planning (BAPLIE) and cargo routing decisions.
Textual explanation for why a scheduled port call was omitted. Common reasons include weather delays, mechanical issues, insufficient cargo, port congestion, or schedule recovery actions.

business_glossary_term: OOG (Out of Gauge) Cargo Onboard Flag
description: Boolean indicator showing whether the vessel is carrying out-of-gauge cargo that exceeds standard container dimensions. Requires special handling equipment and stowage planning considerations.
reference: ISO 668 - Container Dimensions and OOG Classification

description: UN/LOCODE (United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations) of the voyage origin port. Five-character code identifying the port where the voyage originated.
Boolean indicator showing whether pilotage service is required for this voyage. Determined by vessel characteristics, port regulations, and pilotage exemption status.
UN/LOCODE of the port immediately preceding the current port call. Used for cargo manifest reconciliation and vessel movement tracking.

Health quarantine status of the vessel and crew. Not_required indicates no health concerns, pending indicates awaiting health authority inspection, cleared indicates health clearance granted, restricted indicates quarantine measures in effect.
The number of refrigerated containers onboard measured in TEU. Requires special handling, power supply monitoring, and temperature-controlled storage planning.

Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or additional voyage information not captured in structured fields. Used for inter-departmental communication and operational coordination.

Sequential rotation identifier within the service string. Used by shipping lines to track vessel position within a cyclical service route (e.g., rotation 1, rotation 2). Supports schedule adherence analysis.
reference: Shipping Line Service Management - Industry Practice

description: The date when the voyage commenced from the origin port. Represents the actual or planned departure date marking the beginning of the commercial voyage journey.
The total container capacity of the vessel for this voyage measured in TEU. Represents the maximum number of twenty-foot equivalent containers the vessel can carry.
reference: ISO 668 - Container Classification and TEU Standard

description: Boolean indicator showing whether tug assistance is required for berthing and unberthing operations. Based on vessel size, maneuverability, weather conditions, and port regulations.
The externally-known unique voyage number assigned by the shipping line or port authority. This is the business identifier used in operational communications, manifests, and port call documentation.

Current lifecycle status of the voyage. Planned indicates initial voyage planning stage, scheduled indicates confirmed berth and resource allocation, in_progress indicates vessel is actively executing the voyage, completed indicates voyage has concluded with vessel departure, cancelled indicates voyage was terminated before execution, delayed indicates voyage is behind schedule.

Classification of the voyage based on direction and scope. Inbound indicates arrival to the port, outbound indicates departure, coastal indicates domestic port-to-port movement, international indicates cross-border movement, feeder indicates short-sea collection/distribution service, and transshipment indicates cargo transfer voyage.

Reference to the berth allocated for the vessel after departing anchorage. Links anchorage assignment to subsequent berth allocation.
Reference to the port call associated with this anchorage assignment. Links the anchorage to the vessel's overall port visit.
Reference to the vessel assigned to this anchorage. Identifies which vessel is anchored.

Foreign key linking to security.zone. Business justification: Anchorage positions fall within defined port security zones. ISPS requires that vessels at anchor comply with zone-specific security measures. Linking anchorage assignments to the governing security z

business_glossary_term: Actual Anchor Drop Time (ATA - Actual Time of Anchoring)
description: Actual timestamp when the vessel dropped anchor at the anchorage position. Recorded by VTS for operational tracking and performance measurement.

business_glossary_term: Actual Weigh Anchor Time (ATD - Actual Time of Departure from Anchorage)

Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Billing runs are scoped to a legal entity in multi-entity port operators for SAP FI batch posting, GL period management, and statutory reporting. Company code on billing run is required for SAP integr

Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Each billing run executes for a specific billing cycle. The billing_cycle table defines the authoritative billing period. Adding billing_cycle_id allows JOIN to retrieve cycle configuration and remove

Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Billing runs are executed against a specific port tariff version. Tariff version control and billing run audit require knowing which port tariff governed a run — essential when tariff changes mid-peri
Reference to the original billing run ID if this run is a rerun or correction of a previous failed or cancelled run. Null for original runs.
reference: NAVIS N4 Billing Module - Rerun and Correction Procedures

description: Indicates whether this billing run requires manual approval before invoices are released to customers. True for high-value or adjustment runs; false for routine periodic runs.
reference: Internal Financial Controls - Billing Approval Policy

description: Date and time when the billing run was approved for invoice release. Null if approval is not required or not yet granted.
Source system from which billing charges were extracted: NAVIS-N4 (primary TOS), TOPS-EXPERT (alternate TOS), MANUAL (manual entry), or PCS (Port Community System for customs/trade charges).
reference: NAVIS N4 TOS, TOPS Expert TOS, Port Community System

description: Date and time when this billing run record was first created in the system. Represents the initial scheduling or setup of the billing run.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the total invoice value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All invoices in a single run use the same base currency.
Total elapsed time in seconds from run start to run end. Used for performance monitoring and capacity planning of billing batch jobs.
Date and time when the billing run execution completed or terminated. Null if run is still in-progress or suspended.
Number of errors or exceptions encountered during billing run execution. Zero indicates a clean run; non-zero requires review of error logs.
High-level summary description of errors encountered during the billing run. Null if no errors occurred. Detailed error logs are maintained separately.
Date on which the billing run revenue was posted to the General Ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI. Null if GL posting has not yet occurred.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - General Ledger Posting Standards

description: Date and time when invoices from this billing run were released and made available to customers via EDI, email, or customer portal. Null if not yet released.
Date and time when this billing run record was last updated. Tracks any changes to status, approval, or other attributes throughout the run lifecycle.
Reference identifier of the NAVIS N4 TOS charge export batch that sourced the billing data for this run. Provides traceability back to the Terminal Operating System.
reference: NAVIS N4 TOS - Billing Integration Interface

description: Free-text notes or comments entered by billing operators regarding special circumstances, manual adjustments, or issues encountered during the billing run execution.
Externally-known unique reference number for the billing run, formatted as BR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for traceability and audit purposes.
Accounting period (YYYY-MM format) to which revenue from this billing run is recognized for financial reporting and EBITDA calculation purposes.
reference: IFRS 15 - Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: Current lifecycle status of the billing run: scheduled (queued for execution), in-progress (actively processing), completed (successfully finished), failed (error encountered), cancelled (manually aborted), or suspended (paused pending resolution).
reference: NAVIS N4 Billing Module - Run Status Workflow

description: Classification of the billing run execution type: periodic (scheduled monthly/weekly), vessel-call (per vessel departure), ad-hoc (manual trigger), final (end-of-period close), adjustment (correction run), or reversal (cancellation run).
reference: NAVIS N4 Billing Module - Run Type Classification

description: Reference identifier of the SAP S/4HANA FI posting batch where generated invoices were posted for financial accounting. Null if posting has not yet occurred or failed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - Batch Posting Standards

description: Date on which this billing run was originally scheduled to execute. May differ from actual run start date if the run was delayed or rescheduled.
reference: NAVIS N4 Billing Module - Scheduling Configuration

description: Date and time when the billing run execution began processing charges from NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System (TOS). Represents the actual start of the batch job.
Total number of individual charge line items processed and aggregated into invoices during this billing run.
reference: NAVIS N4 Billing Module - Charge Processing Metrics

description: Distinct count of customer accounts that received at least one invoice in this billing run.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - Accounts Receivable Reporting

description: Aggregate monetary value of all invoices generated in this billing run, representing gross revenue before adjustments. Used for EBITDA-level revenue reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - Financial Accounting Standards

description: Total number of invoices successfully generated and created during this billing run execution.
reference: NAVIS N4 Billing Module - Invoice Generation Metrics

description: Unique identifier for the payment record. Primary key for the payment entity.

Foreign key linking to booking.cargo_booking. Business justification: Freight forwarders and shippers pre-pay THC and cargo handling charges against cargo bookings before vessel arrival. Port finance teams must link advance cargo payments to the originating cargo bookin

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Payment cost center attribution enables cash collection performance tracking by operational unit. Port finance teams measure collection efficiency by terminal or service division for working capital m

Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Payments are allocated to billing cycles for period accounting and cash flow reporting. This FK enables cycle-based payment analysis. Keeps fiscal_year and fiscal_period on payment as execution-specif

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Cash receipts post to bank GL accounts for cash accounting. Daily bank reconciliation process requires linking payments to specific bank account GL codes for treasury management and cash flow reportin
Reference to the invoice against which this payment is applied. Links payment to billing document.

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Cash receipt postings create journal entries in the GL; linking payment to its journal entry is required for bank reconciliation, audit trail, and SAP FI cash application reporting. Port treasury team
Reference to the port community participant account making the payment. Identifies the payer.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Cash receipts attributed to profit centers for cash-basis divisional reporting. Port authorities track cash realization by business segment for liquidity planning and capital allocation decisions.

Foreign key linking to billing.receivable_account. Business justification: A payment is made against a customer's receivable account — the receivable_account is the authoritative AR master for each port community participant in the billing domain. Linking payment.receivable_
Portion of the payment amount that has been allocated to specific invoices. May be less than amount_paid if payment is partially applied or held on account.
Total amount paid in the transaction currency. Represents the gross payment received before any adjustments or allocations.
Bank transaction reference or confirmation number provided by the financial institution. Used for payment tracing and dispute resolution.
Payment amount converted to the port's base reporting currency using the exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting and EBITDA calculation.
Channel or interface through which the payment was submitted (online portal, bank transfer, Port Community System, SAP, manual entry, or Electronic Data Interchange).
Date when the payment was cleared and funds were confirmed available in the port's bank account. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Date and time when this payment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, SGD).
Early payment discount amount claimed by the customer and deducted from the invoice total. Reflects payment terms incentives.
Reference number for payment dispute case if reconciliation status is disputed. Links to dispute resolution workflow.
Exchange rate applied to convert payment currency to port base currency. Used for multi-currency payment reconciliation and financial reporting.
Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the payment was received. Used for monthly financial close and reporting.
Fiscal year in which the payment was received. Used for annual financial reporting and revenue recognition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - Fiscal Year Variant

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this is an advance payment received before services are rendered. True if advance, False otherwise.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - Special GL Indicator

description: Date and time when this payment record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Reference to the NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System billing transaction that generated the invoice. Links payment back to operational billing source.
Free-text notes or comments related to the payment. May include remittance advice, special instructions, or reconciliation remarks.
Bank account number from which the payment originated. Used for payment verification and reconciliation. Business-confidential financial information.
Name of the financial institution from which the payment was sent. Used for payment tracing and reconciliation.
Date when the payment was received or processed by the port authority. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Method or instrument used to make the payment. Indicates how funds were transferred (Electronic Funds Transfer, SWIFT wire, cheque, direct debit, letter of credit, or cash).
Current status of the payment in the accounts receivable lifecycle. Tracks payment from receipt through clearing and application to invoices.
Classification of payment purpose. Distinguishes between advance payments, payments on account, invoice settlements, refunds, and credit note applications.
Date and time when the payment was first received or recorded in the system. Principal business event timestamp. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Date and time when the payment was reconciled. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Status of payment reconciliation against bank statements and invoice records. Tracks whether payment has been matched and verified.
External payment reference number provided by the payer or payment system. Used for reconciliation and customer inquiries.
Reference number from the remittance advice document sent by the payer. Links payment to payer's accounts payable records.
Date when the payment was reversed or bounced. Null if payment has not been reversed. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Explanation for payment reversal if payment status is reversed. Documents reason for chargeback, NSF (non-sufficient funds), or cancellation.
SAP FI clearing document number generated when payment is applied against open invoice items. Links to SAP accounts receivable clearing process.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - Document Number Range

description: SAP FI payment document number created during payment posting. Primary reference for financial audit trail in SAP S/4HANA.
Portion of the payment amount not yet allocated to invoices. Held on account for future invoice settlement or refund.
Unique identifier for the payment allocation record. Primary key for the payment allocation entity.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Payment allocations clear AR receivables; cost centre attribution is required for cash application reporting and management accounting reconciliation in port finance. The denormalized cost_center plai

Foreign key linking to billing.debit_note. Business justification: Payment allocations can be applied against debit notes (additional charges). This FK enables tracking of payments allocated to debit notes, completing the payment allocation model (currently links to

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Payment allocations may hit different GL accounts for discounts, write-offs, or foreign exchange variances. Variance accounting requires GL-level tracking of allocation adjustments for financial state
Reference to the invoice receiving the payment allocation. Links to the invoice record generated from NAVIS N4 billing module.
Reference to the specific invoice line item receiving the allocation. Nullable when allocation applies to entire invoice rather than specific line.

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Payment allocations create clearing journal entries in SAP FI when AR items are cleared; linking payment_allocation to its journal entry supports bank reconciliation, audit trail, and period-end AR cl
Reference to the payment transaction being allocated. Links to the payment record in the payment processing system.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Profit centre on payment allocations supports segment cash collection reporting — port operators need to track which business unit collected cash for treasury and segment reporting. The denormalized p
Financial period (YYYY-MM format) to which this allocation is posted. Critical for period-end close, revenue recognition, and financial reporting.
The monetary amount allocated from the payment to the invoice or invoice line. Represents the portion of payment applied to reduce outstanding balance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Amount in Document Currency

description: The date when the payment was allocated to the invoice. Critical for accounts receivable aging and financial period assignment.
Business reference number for the payment allocation transaction. Used for tracking and reconciliation purposes across systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Clearing Document Number

description: System or process that created the allocation. Manual: user-entered; Automated: system-matched; EDI: electronic data interchange; Lockbox: bank lockbox service; API: external system integration.

Current processing status of the allocation. Pending: awaiting confirmation; Confirmed: successfully applied; Reversed: allocation undone; Cancelled: allocation voided; Disputed: under review for billing dispute.
Precise date and time when the allocation was processed in the system. Used for audit trail and reconciliation timing.

Classification of the allocation transaction. Full: entire invoice paid; Partial: portion of invoice paid; Advance: payment before invoice; Overpayment: payment exceeds invoice; On-account: unallocated payment held for future invoices; Credit-note: allocation from credit memo.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Allocation Types

description: Reference number from bank statement used to match and reconcile this allocation. Critical for automated cash application and bank reconciliation processes.
Business area code for cross-company financial reporting. Enables consolidated financial statements across multiple legal entities.
SAP company code representing the legal entity for which the allocation is recorded. Critical for multi-entity financial consolidation.
System timestamp when the allocation record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the allocated amount. Supports multi-currency payment allocation for international trade transactions.
Early payment discount amount deducted during allocation. Reflects payment terms incentives such as 2/10 net 30. Reduces allocated amount but clears full invoice value.
Indicates whether this allocation is under dispute. True when customer has raised a billing dispute affecting this allocation. Impacts accounts receivable aging classification.
Reference to the billing dispute case if allocation is disputed. Links to dispute resolution workflow. Nullable when no dispute exists.
Currency exchange rate applied when payment currency differs from invoice currency. Used for multi-currency allocation and foreign exchange gain/loss calculation.
Fiscal year to which the allocation belongs. Supports financial reporting and year-end close processes.
Allocated amount converted to the company code local currency using the exchange rate. Used for financial reporting in local GAAP.
User ID or system account that last modified the allocation record. Supports audit trail and change tracking.
System timestamp when the allocation record was last updated. Tracks changes for audit and reconciliation purposes.
Free-text notes or comments regarding the allocation. Used for documenting special circumstances, manual adjustments, or reconciliation details.
The remaining unpaid balance on the invoice after this allocation is applied. Zero indicates invoice is fully paid. Critical for accounts receivable aging reports.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Open Item Management

description: Date when the allocation was reversed or cancelled. Nullable for active allocations. Used for audit trail and financial period correction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reversal Posting Date

description: SAP FI document number of the reversal transaction. Links to the offsetting entry that undoes the original allocation. Nullable for non-reversed allocations.
Business justification for reversing or cancelling the allocation. Nullable when allocation is not reversed. Examples: payment error, invoice dispute, incorrect amount, duplicate payment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reversal Reason Codes

description: SAP S/4HANA FI clearing document number that links the payment and invoice in the financial system. Critical for financial close and reconciliation.
Line item number within the SAP clearing document. Enables precise traceability to specific line-level clearing entries in SAP FI.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Clearing Line Item

description: Tax amount withheld at source during payment allocation as per jurisdictional tax regulations. Common in international maritime trade transactions.
Unique identifier for the receivable account. Primary key for the customer billing and accounts receivable master record.

Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_broker. Business justification: Customs brokers are primary billing parties at ports, holding receivable accounts for customs-related charges on behalf of importers/exporters. Port finance teams maintain receivable accounts directly

Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Each customer receivable account has a default billing cycle. The receivable_account table has billing_cycle (STRING), and billing_cycle table has cycle_code. Adding billing_cycle_id FK enables JOIN t
Unique identifier for this customer in the port's EDI network. Used for automated invoice transmission via EDIFACT or X12 protocols.

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each AR receivable account maps to a reconciliation GL account in the sub-ledger; this is a standard SAP FI-AR integration requirement. Port finance teams use this link for AR sub-ledger to GL reconci

Foreign key linking to intermodal.haulier. Business justification: Hauliers are billed customers for drayage and gate services. Ports maintain receivable accounts for hauliers to track outstanding balances, credit limits, payment terms, and aging. Essential for credi

Foreign key linking to intermodal.icd_facility. Business justification: ICD facilities are billed entities requiring dedicated AR accounts for storage, customs examination, and handling charges. receivable_account already tracks haulier and rail_operator AR; ICD facility
Reference to the parent receivable account if this is a subsidiary or branch account. Enables hierarchical billing and consolidated statements.

Foreign key linking to customer.participant_account. Business justification: AR account management: every receivable_account in port billing maps to one participant_account for credit control, dunning, and collections. Maritime AR teams reconcile aging buckets and outstanding

Foreign key linking to intermodal.rail_operator. Business justification: Rail operators are billed for terminal services (loading, unloading, storage, track usage). Ports maintain receivable accounts to manage rail operator billing, credit terms, payment tracking, and dunn

Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card. Business justification: Receivable accounts for contracted customers are associated with a specific rate card governing their billing terms. Customer account management, credit limit setting, and auto-billing configuration r

Foreign key linking to masterdata.shipping_line. Business justification: Port AR credit management, carrier-level aging reports, and dunning processes require receivable accounts to be directly linked to shipping line master records. Maritime AR teams manage credit limits
Business classification of the account holder based on their role in the port community ecosystem. Determines applicable tariff structures and service entitlements.
Date when this receivable account was closed or terminated. Null for active accounts.
Business-assigned unique alphanumeric code identifying the receivable account. Used for operational reference and reporting.
Legal or trading name of the customer organization or individual holding this receivable account.
Date when this receivable account was first established and activated in the billing system.
Current lifecycle status of the receivable account. Active accounts can transact; suspended/blocked accounts require approval; closed accounts are archived.
Total outstanding balance for invoices aged 0 to 30 days past due date. Used for accounts receivable aging analysis and collections prioritization.
Total outstanding balance for invoices aged 31 to 60 days past due date. Indicates moderate collection risk.
Total outstanding balance for invoices aged 61 to 90 days past due date. Indicates elevated collection risk requiring escalation.
Total outstanding balance for invoices aged over 90 days past due date. Indicates high collection risk; may require legal action or write-off consideration.
Indicates whether this account is enrolled in automatic payment via direct debit or standing order. True means invoices are auto-settled on due date.
Rolling average number of days from invoice date to payment receipt over the last 12 months. Key metric for credit risk assessment.
First line of the postal address to which invoices and statements are sent. Typically contains street number and name.
Second line of the billing postal address. Used for suite/floor/building details or additional address information.
City or municipality component of the billing address.
Three-letter ISO country code for the billing address country.
Primary email address for sending invoices, statements, and billing-related communications.
Primary phone number for billing inquiries and dispute resolution. Format varies by country.
Postal or ZIP code component of the billing address. Format varies by country.
State, province, or region component of the billing address. Format varies by country.
Indicates whether invoices for this account should be consolidated with parent account for statement purposes. True enables group-level billing.
Timestamp when this receivable account record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field.
Indicates whether this account is currently on credit hold. True means no new services can be rendered until outstanding balance is reduced.
Maximum outstanding balance allowed for this account before credit hold is triggered. Expressed in the account's base currency.

Internal or external credit rating assessment for this account. Used to determine credit limits and payment terms. D indicates default risk. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D|unrated — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Number of billing disputes raised by this account in the current calendar year. High counts indicate service quality or billing accuracy issues.
Current collections escalation level (0=none, 1=reminder, 2=warning, 3=final notice, 4=legal action). Increments with each overdue period.
Preferred channel for delivering invoices to the customer. EDI is common for large shipping lines; email for smaller customers.
Date of the most recent invoice issued to this account. Used to identify dormant accounts.
Amount of the most recent payment received from this account in the account's base currency.
Date of the most recent payment received from this account. Used to calculate average days to pay and payment behavior trends.
Timestamp when this receivable account record was last updated. Updated automatically on any field change.
Current total amount owed by the customer across all unpaid invoices. Updated in real-time as invoices are issued and payments are received.

Standard payment terms defining the number of days from invoice date until payment is due. Net 7/14/30/60 are common port industry terms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_7|net_14|net_30|net_60|net_90|due_on_receipt|cod — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for invoicing this account. All invoices will be issued in this currency unless overridden at transaction level.
SAP S/4HANA customer master number (KNA1/KNB1) linking this receivable account to the ERP financial accounting module. Ten-digit numeric identifier.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Customer Master Data

description: Frequency at which account statements summarizing all invoices and payments are generated and sent to the customer.
Three-letter ISO country code representing the primary tax jurisdiction for this account. Determines applicable tax rates and compliance requirements.
Tax registration identifier for VAT/GST purposes. Required for invoicing and tax compliance reporting. Format varies by jurisdiction.
Total amount written off as bad debt for this account in the current calendar year. Impacts credit rating and future credit decisions.
Unique identifier for the statement of account record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Statements of account are issued per legal entity in multi-entity port operators; company code scoping is required for statutory AR reporting and customer communication from the correct legal entity.

Reference to the customer receivable account for which this statement is issued. Links to the billing account master.
Self-referencing FK on statement_of_account (prior_statement_of_account_id)

Reference to the billing run batch process during which this statement was generated as part of the month-end or period-end billing close.
The name of the customer contact person who acknowledged the statement on behalf of the customer organization.
The date on which the customer formally acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the statement.
Indicates whether the customer has acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the statement, raised a dispute, or has not responded.
The portion of the closing balance representing invoices that are overdue by 31-60 days from their due date.
The portion of the closing balance representing invoices that are overdue by 61-90 days from their due date.
The portion of the closing balance representing invoices that are current or overdue by 0-30 days from their due date.
The portion of the closing balance representing invoices that are overdue by more than 90 days from their due date. Indicates high collection risk.
The date on which the statement was cancelled, if applicable.
The business reason for cancelling the statement, if the statement status is cancelled. Used for audit and process improvement.
The outstanding accounts receivable balance at the end of the statement period. Calculated as opening balance plus invoices and debit adjustments minus payments and credit adjustments.
The date and time when the statement record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.
The total number of credit adjustment transactions applied to the customer account during the statement period.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all monetary amounts on this statement are denominated.
The total number of debit adjustment transactions applied to the customer account during the statement period.
The channel through which the statement was delivered to the customer. Aligns with customer communication preferences.
The date and time when the statement was successfully delivered to the customer through the specified delivery method.
Indicates whether the customer has raised a dispute or discrepancy regarding the statement contents or balances.
The unique reference number of the billing dispute case raised by the customer in relation to this statement, if applicable.
The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year in which the statement period falls.
The fiscal year in which the statement period falls, used for financial reporting and period alignment.
The total number of invoices issued to the customer during the statement period.
The date and time when the statement record was last modified in the system. Audit trail for record updates.
Free-text field for capturing additional remarks, special instructions, or contextual information related to the statement.
The outstanding accounts receivable balance at the beginning of the statement period. Represents carried-forward balance from prior period.
The total number of payment transactions received from the customer during the statement period.
The SAP S/4HANA FI document number associated with the statement generation or posting transaction in the financial system.
The date on which the statement was generated and issued to the customer. Represents the as-of date for all balances and transactions included.
The periodic cadence at which this statement is generated for the customer account. Aligns with billing cycle configuration.
Externally visible unique statement reference number issued to the customer. Format: SOA-YYYYMMDD-NNNNNN.
Current lifecycle status of the statement. Tracks progression from draft through issuance, customer acknowledgement, and closure.
The aggregate value of all credit notes, credit memos, and favorable adjustments applied to the customer account during the statement period.
The aggregate value of all debit notes, debit memos, and unfavorable adjustments applied to the customer account during the statement period.
The aggregate value of all invoices issued to the customer during the statement period. Increases the receivable balance.
The aggregate value of all payments received from the customer during the statement period. Reduces the receivable balance.

The date on which the accrual was fully settled or cleared against an actual transaction. Nullable for open accruals.
Indicates whether the accrual has been settled against an actual invoice or payment. Used to track the lifecycle from accrual to final settlement.
The difference between the accrued amount and the actual invoiced or settled amount. Used for variance analysis and accrual accuracy assessment.
Unique identifier for the company code entity. Primary key. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE (legal entity master).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Master Data

description: Date when the company code was activated in the ERP system and became available for financial postings. Determines the earliest valid posting date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Configuration

description: Boolean flag indicating whether business area assignment is mandatory for all financial postings. Used for cross-company code segment reporting and divisional analysis.

description: Official registration number assigned by the corporate registry or chamber of commerce. Used for legal entity verification, trade documentation, and regulatory filings.

description: Four-character alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the legal entity within the corporate group. Used as the primary business identifier in SAP FI/CO and all financial transactions. Examples: PAUS (Port Authority US), TCSG (Terminal Container Singapore).

description: Code identifying the consolidation group or reporting segment to which this entity belongs. Used for segment reporting under IFRS 8 and management consolidation.
