Indicates whether the carrier is approved and certified to handle IMDG (International Maritime Dangerous Goods) cargo at this port. Requires specific documentation, training, and compliance with IMDG Code.

Business unit responsible for maintaining and validating the accuracy of this shipping line master record. Commercial: handles tariff and contract data. Marine Operations: handles vessel scheduling and operational data. Both: shared responsibility.
Indicates whether the carrier has active EDI integration with the port for automated message exchange. True: EDI active. False: manual/email-based communication.

value_regex: Mega Carrier|Major Carrier|Regional Carrier|Niche Carrier|Feeder Operator
description: Classification of the carrier based on global fleet capacity measured in TEU. Mega Carrier: >1M TEU. Major Carrier: 500K-1M TEU. Regional Carrier: 100K-500K TEU. Niche Carrier: 10K-100K TEU. Feeder Operator: <10K TEU. Used for berth allocation priority and commercial negotiation.
City where the shipping line's global or regional headquarters is located.

business_glossary_term: ISO (International Organization for Standardization) Certification Status
description: Highest level of ISO certification held by the carrier. ISO 9001: Quality Management. ISO 14001: Environmental Management. ISO 28000: Supply Chain Security. Multiple: holds more than one certification. Used for vendor assessment and partnership evaluation.
reference: ISO Standards; International Organization for Standardization

business_glossary_term: ISPS (International Ship and Port Facility Security) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the carrier maintains valid ISPS certification and compliance for all vessels calling at the port. Required for international vessel operations per SOLAS Chapter XI-2.
reference: ISPS Code (SOLAS Chapter XI-2); International Maritime Organization (IMO)

description: Date when this shipping line master record was last reviewed and validated for accuracy by the data steward. Used for data quality monitoring and compliance.

Current operational state of the shipping line at this port. Active: currently operating vessel calls. Suspended: temporarily not calling at port. Ceased: permanently discontinued operations. Merged/Acquired: absorbed into another carrier entity.
Standard number of days allowed for payment of port charges and terminal handling charges (THC) after invoice date. Typical values: 0 (prepaid), 7, 14, 30, 45, 60 days.
reference: Commercial Contract Terms; SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms

description: Preferred advance notice window in hours between ETB (Estimated Time of Berthing) and vessel arrival that the carrier requests for berth allocation planning. Used by berth planning system to optimize scheduling.
reference: NAVIS N4 Vessel Planning Module; Port Operations Standard

description: Email address of the primary contact for operational communications, vessel scheduling, and commercial matters.
reference: GDPR Article 4; Internal Contact Management Standard

description: Full name of the principal commercial or operations contact person at the shipping line for port coordination and service matters.
Direct telephone number of the primary contact including country and area codes for urgent operational coordination.
reference: GDPR Article 4; Internal Contact Management Standard

description: Timestamp when this shipping line master record was first created in the system.
User identifier or system process name that last modified this shipping line master record. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Timestamp when this shipping line master record was last modified. Updated on any field change.
Indicates whether the carrier operates reefer-capable vessels and handles refrigerated cargo at this port. Impacts berth allocation to reefer-equipped berths.
reference: Port Operations Standard; NAVIS N4 Vessel Planning

description: Four-letter unique identifier assigned by the National Motor Freight Traffic Association (NMFTA) to identify ocean carriers in EDI transactions and shipping documentation. Required for all US-bound cargo per CBP regulations.
reference: NMFTA SCAC Standard; US Customs and Border Protection (CBP)

description: Date when the shipping line first commenced vessel operations at this port facility.
Date when the shipping line ceased or suspended vessel operations at this port. Null for active carriers.

Primary type of shipping service the carrier provides at this port. Mainline: direct long-haul international routes. Feeder: short-sea distribution services. Transshipment Hub: hub-and-spoke operations. Regional: intra-regional services. Specialized: project cargo, heavy lift, or niche services.
Internal code linking the carrier to a specific tariff schedule for port charges, THC (Terminal Handling Charges), wharfage, and other fees. Carriers in the same group receive identical pricing.
reference: NAVIS N4 Billing Module; Port Tariff Management System

business_glossary_term: Total Fleet TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit) Capacity
description: Total container capacity of the carrier's global fleet measured in TEU. Used for commercial assessment and berth planning.
Total number of vessels (owned and chartered) in the carrier's operating fleet. Includes container ships, RoRo vessels, and specialized cargo vessels.

Preferred method for VGM verification per SOLAS Chapter VI Regulation 2. Method 1: weighing the packed container. Method 2: weighing all contents and adding tare weight. Both: carrier accepts either method. Required for all export containers since July 2016.
reference: SOLAS Chapter VI Regulation 2; International Maritime Organization (IMO)

description: Official corporate website URL of the shipping line for reference and public information access.
Unique system identifier for the vessel master record. Primary key for the vessel master entity.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.flag_state. Business justification: Vessel master records reference flag state (country of vessel registration). Currently stores flag_state_code as STRING; normalizing to FK allows joining to flag_state for full regulatory compliance d

business_glossary_term: Port Of Registry Location Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Vessel master records reference the port of registry (home port) as a location. Currently stores port_of_registry as STRING; normalizing to FK allows joining to port_location for full location data (U

Foreign key linking to masterdata.shipping_line. Business justification: Vessel master records reference the shipping line (ocean carrier) that commercially operates the vessel. Currently stores commercial_operator_code as STRING; normalizing to FK allows joining to shippi

Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: Vessel master records reference vessel type classification (container ship, bulk carrier, etc.). Currently stores vessel_type_code as STRING; normalizing to FK allows joining to vessel_type for full t
Maximum width of the vessel at its widest point. Used for berth compatibility assessment and safe navigation channel clearance calculations.
reference: International Convention on Tonnage Measurement of Ships

description: Name of the shipyard or shipbuilding company that constructed the vessel. Relevant for technical specifications and spare parts sourcing.
International radio call sign assigned to the vessel for maritime communication and identification in Vessel Traffic Service (VTS) operations.
Code identifying the classification society that certifies the vessel meets structural and mechanical standards (e.g., Lloyd's Register, DNV, ABS). Impacts insurance and port acceptance.
Timestamp when this vessel master record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Business unit or role responsible for maintaining the accuracy and completeness of this vessel master record. Typically Marine Operations department.
Type and model of the vessel's main propulsion engine. Used for emissions tracking, fuel consumption estimation, and environmental compliance under MARPOL.
Maximum number of forty-foot equivalent containers the vessel can carry. Supplementary capacity metric for container vessel planning.
Total internal volume of the vessel measured in register tons (100 cubic feet). Used for regulatory compliance, port dues calculation, and statistical reporting.
reference: International Convention on Tonnage Measurement of Ships 1969

description: Shipyard construction number assigned during vessel building. Used for tracking vessel lineage and construction specifications.
Classification indicating the vessel's capability to operate in ice-covered waters. Relevant for seasonal port access and route planning in northern latitudes.
reference: IACS Unified Requirements for Polar Ships

description: Unique seven-digit ship identification number assigned by the International Maritime Organization. Permanent identifier that remains unchanged through changes of name, ownership, or flag. SSOT for global vessel identity.
Boolean flag indicating whether this is the current active version of the vessel master record. True for the latest version, False for historical versions.

business_glossary_term: International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Compliant Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether the vessel holds a valid International Ship Security Certificate (ISSC) and complies with ISPS Code requirements. Mandatory for port entry.

business_glossary_term: International Ship Security Certificate (ISSC) Expiry Date
description: Expiration date of the vessel's International Ship Security Certificate. Vessels with expired certificates may be denied port entry or subjected to enhanced Port State Control inspection.

business_glossary_term: Last Port State Control (PSC) Inspection Date
description: Date of the most recent Port State Control inspection. Used for risk assessment and targeting of vessels for inspection under regional PSC memoranda of understanding.
reference: Paris MOU / Tokyo MOU on Port State Control

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this vessel master record. Used for data freshness monitoring and change data capture processing.
Unique identifier assigned by Lloyd's List Intelligence maritime data service. Used for cross-referencing vessel data with external maritime intelligence feeds.
Maximum length of the vessel measured from the foremost point of the bow to the aftermost point of the stern. Critical dimension for berth allocation and port infrastructure planning.
reference: International Convention on Tonnage Measurement of Ships

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the vessel complies with MARPOL convention requirements for prevention of pollution from ships (oil, chemicals, sewage, garbage, air emissions).
Maximum vertical distance between the waterline and the bottom of the hull (keel). Determines minimum water depth requirements for safe berthing and navigation.
Nine-digit unique identifier used in the Automatic Identification System (AIS) and Digital Selective Calling (DSC) for vessel tracking and communication.
Volume of cargo-carrying spaces measured in register tons. Represents earning capacity and is used for port tariff and canal dues calculation.
reference: International Convention on Tonnage Measurement of Ships 1969

description: Current operational state of the vessel. Active vessels are in service; laid-up vessels are temporarily out of service; scrapped vessels are decommissioned; under construction vessels are not yet delivered; detained vessels are held by authorities.
Code identifying the Protection and Indemnity insurance club providing third-party liability coverage for the vessel. Required for port entry and cargo operations.
Maximum continuous rated power output of the main propulsion engine in kilowatts. Used for environmental reporting and vessel performance assessment.
Total number of deficiencies identified during the last PSC inspection. High deficiency counts indicate elevated risk and may trigger enhanced inspection or detention.
reference: Paris MOU / Tokyo MOU on Port State Control

description: Legal entity name of the registered owner as recorded in the ship registry. May differ from commercial operator. Subject to change through ownership transfers tracked via effectivity dating.
reference: United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS)

business_glossary_term: Safety of Life at Sea (SOLAS) Compliant Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether the vessel meets SOLAS convention requirements for construction, equipment, and operation. Fundamental requirement for international maritime operations.

Name of the operational system or external feed that provided this vessel master data (e.g., Lloyd's List Intelligence, NAVIS N4, Vessel Traffic Management System, manual entry via Port Community System).
Maximum weight in metric tons that the vessel can safely carry (cargo, fuel, water, stores, crew) when loaded to the summer load line. Key metric for cargo capacity and port tariff calculation.
Maximum number of twenty-foot equivalent containers the vessel can carry. Primary capacity metric for container vessels used in terminal planning and vessel scheduling.
Effective start date for this version of the vessel master record. Supports Type 2 slowly changing dimension tracking for vessel name changes, ownership transfers, and flag changes.
Effective end date for this version of the vessel master record. Null indicates the current active record. Supports historical analysis and audit trail for vessel attribute changes.
Current registered name of the vessel as recorded in the ship registry. Subject to change through vessel name amendments tracked via effectivity dating.
reference: International Convention on Tonnage Measurement of Ships

description: Calendar year in which the vessel was constructed and delivered. Used for age-based risk assessment, insurance classification, and Port State Control (PSC) targeting.
Primary key for vessel_type
Indicates whether this vessel type has specific berth compatibility requirements that must be validated during berth allocation.
Primary cargo handling method associated with this vessel type: Lift-on Lift-off (LoLo), Roll-on Roll-off (RoRo), bulk discharge, liquid pumping, or mixed methods.
Timestamp when this vessel type classification record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether vessels of this type are certified and equipped to carry dangerous goods as defined by IMDG Code.
Name or identifier of the business unit or individual responsible for maintaining the accuracy and currency of this vessel type classification record. Typically Marine Operations.
Environmental classification of vessel type based on typical emissions profile and environmental impact. Used for GHG reporting and environmental compliance.
reference: IMO GHG Strategy and MARPOL Annex VI

business_glossary_term: International Maritime Organization (IMO) Vessel Type Code
description: Two-digit IMO standard vessel type code as defined by the International Maritime Organization for global vessel classification.

business_glossary_term: International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Security Level
description: Default ISPS security level applicable to vessels of this type: Level 1 (normal), Level 2 (heightened), Level 3 (exceptional), or not applicable.
reference: ISPS Code (International Ship and Port Facility Security)

description: Timestamp when this vessel type classification record was last modified or updated.
Comma-separated list of applicable MARPOL annex references for this vessel type (e.g., Annex I - Oil, Annex II - Noxious Liquid Substances).
Indicates whether vessels of this type are compatible with mobile harbour cranes (MHC) for cargo handling operations.
NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System vessel category code used for vessel planning, berth allocation, and operational scheduling.
reference: NAVIS N4 TOS Vessel Planning Module

description: Numeric priority ranking for berth allocation and scheduling when multiple vessels compete for limited berth capacity. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.
Indicates whether vessels of this type typically require marine pilotage services for port entry and berthing operations.
Indicates whether vessels of this type typically require towage (tug) services for berthing and unberthing operations.

business_glossary_term: Safety of Life at Sea (SOLAS) Chapter Reference
description: Comma-separated list of applicable SOLAS chapter references for this vessel type (e.g., Chapter II-1, Chapter II-2, Chapter V).
reference: SOLAS Convention (Safety of Life at Sea)

description: Indicates whether vessels of this type are compatible with ship-to-shore (STS) quay cranes for container handling operations.
Tariff category code used to determine applicable port charges, wharfage, and terminal handling charges (THC) for this vessel type.
Maximum typical beam (width) in meters for vessels of this type. Used for berth width compatibility assessment.
Minimum typical beam (width) in meters for vessels of this type. Used for berth width compatibility assessment.
Maximum typical draft (depth below waterline) in meters for vessels of this type. Critical for channel and berth depth compatibility.
Minimum typical draft (depth below waterline) in meters for vessels of this type. Critical for channel and berth depth compatibility.
Maximum typical deadweight tonnage (DWT) in metric tons for vessels of this type. Used for berth compatibility and tariff calculation.
Minimum typical deadweight tonnage (DWT) in metric tons for vessels of this type. Used for berth compatibility and tariff calculation.
Maximum typical gross registered tonnage (GRT) for vessels of this type. Used for regulatory reporting and tariff calculations.
Minimum typical gross registered tonnage (GRT) for vessels of this type. Used for regulatory reporting and tariff calculations.

business_glossary_term: Typical Length Overall (LOA) Maximum in Meters
description: Maximum typical length overall (LOA) in meters for vessels of this type. Critical for berth allocation and planning.

business_glossary_term: Typical Length Overall (LOA) Minimum in Meters
description: Minimum typical length overall (LOA) in meters for vessels of this type. Critical for berth allocation and planning.

business_glossary_term: Typical Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit (TEU) Capacity Maximum
description: Maximum typical container capacity in TEU for container vessel types. Null for non-container vessel types.

business_glossary_term: Typical Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit (TEU) Capacity Minimum
description: Minimum typical container capacity in TEU for container vessel types. Null for non-container vessel types.
Effective start date from which this vessel type classification is valid and available for operational use. Supports temporal versioning of classification changes.
Effective end date after which this vessel type classification is no longer valid. Null indicates the classification is currently active with no planned end date.

High-level categorical grouping of vessel types for operational and analytical purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: container|bulk|tanker|roro|lolo|general_cargo|passenger|tug|barge|specialized — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the vessel type classification. Used as business key across operational systems.
Detailed textual description of the vessel type classification, including operational characteristics, typical cargo types, and special handling requirements.
Full descriptive name of the vessel type classification (e.g., Container Ship, Bulk Carrier, Tanker, RoRo, LoLo, General Cargo, Passenger/Cruise, Tug, Barge).
Current lifecycle status of the vessel type classification record. Active types are available for operational use; deprecated types are retained for historical reference only.

business_glossary_term: Vessel Traffic Service (VTS) Tracking Required Flag
description: Indicates whether vessels of this type are required to be tracked by the Vessel Traffic Service (VTS) system during port operations.
